What if there were 24 beavers caught each time and 8 were tagged the second time? Assuming that we are catching a random set of beavers each time, and none were tagged before we caught the first group, then there are about 72 beavers in the population. Here's why: The second time through, 1/3 of the beavers caught were tagged. Since it's a random set from the population, then about 1/3 of the population should be tagged. Since none of the population was tagged before we got there, and we only tagged 24, then 24 must be 1/3 of the total population. solve for P: This should get you going on your problem. [moving objects on big magnet by holding two balls slightly above them.] [looks in the pocket, pulls out some clothes] Columbus was not the first European person to have discovered America. At the time of his voyage, Europeans did not know that the Americas existed. However, Leif Erikson, around 1000 AD had landed in present-day Canada. This discovery had no impact on European history and was not well documented. Columbus discovered America in the sense that he was the first person to create repeated exploration and contact with the New World. Another point is that Native Americans had been living there for thousands of years before he arrived. However, Native Americans did not record or contribute to the European record of history for obvious reasons. Columbus, therefore, "discovered" America in context of European history. Fill in the numbers 1 through n in every row and in every column. Next to the puzzle the value of n is given. Numbers indicate the sum, the product, the difference or the result of the division of all the numbers in that region. Often the operator used is given. Subtraction and division can only apply to regions with two numbers. It is possible for numbers to occur more than once within a region as long as this does not violate any of the rules. The example puzzle requires us to fill in the numbers 1, 2, 3 and 4 in every row and every column. In the rightmost column there is only one place where the 4 can be, because 4 is not an integer divisor of 6 and 2. In the same column the 3 is not an integer divisor of 2, but it is of 6, so we write the 3 above the 4. Since 2x3=6, the cell the left of this 3 needs to be a 2. Because 3+4=7, the cell to the left of the 4 needs to be a 3. The first row still needs to have a 1 and a 4. Although we don't know yet in what order they need to be, we do know they add up to 5 and that together with another cell they add up to 6. This means this extra cell on the second row and in the second column must be a 1. Now because there can only be one 1 in the second column, row one becomes 1423. The second row can also be completed, because only the 2 is still missing. Now the first column can be completed to 1234, and to the right of the 4 we need to write a 3, because 4+3=7. Then the second column can be completed to 4123. The third column still needs a 1 and a 4, but there is already a 4 on the fourth row, so the order becomes 2341. The rightmost column will have to be 3412. This type of puzzle was invented by Tetsuya Miyamoto, a Japanese math teacher. It is also known under the trademarked names KenKen(R) and KenDoku(R). Puzzles in this genre My bath must be ready by now. Title: How to Dress for a Fair: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Fairs are joyous occasions filled with games, rides, food, and entertainment. To fully enjoy your day at the fair, dressing appropriately is crucial. This guide will help you choose an outfit that balances comfort, style, and protection from the elements. Step 1: Check the Weather Forecast Before deciding on an outfit, check the weather forecast for the day of the fair. This information will influence your clothing choices. If it's going to be hot, opt for lightweight fabrics; if there's rain predicted, pack waterproof layers. Step 2: Choose Comfortable Footwear Comfortable shoes are essential when attending a fair due to extensive walking and potential uneven terrain. Sneakers or boots with good arch support are ideal. Avoid open-toed sandals as they can lead to discomfort or injury on rough surfaces or wet grounds. Step 3: Opt for Light Layers Temperatures can fluctuate throughout the day, especially if you're visiting amusement park rides where wind chill may occur. Breathable layered pieces such as t-shirts, cardigans, or jackets allow adjustment according to these changes. Step 4: Prioritize Movement Freedom Avoid restrictive clothes that hinder movement. Instead, go for loose-fitting garments or stretchy materials like jeans, leggings, or skirts. Remember, you might need to sit down quickly to rest or get onto certain rides. Step 5: Protect Your Skin Sunscreen should always be part of your fair attire, regardless of the forecast. Apply broad-spectrum sunscreen with at least SPF 30 before leaving home and reapply every two hours, more often if sweating heavily or swimming. Also consider wearing a hat to shield your face and sunglasses to protect your eyes. Step 6: Accessorize Wisely Jewelry can add flair to your look but could pose safety risks near moving parts or during fast rides. Keep accessories minimal and secure. For instance, wear stud earrings instead of dangly ones, and avoid necklaces that could snag. Key Tips and Guidelines: - Closed-toe shoes are safer than sandals or flip-flops. - Dressing in layers allows flexibility against varying temperatures. - Always prioritize comfort over fashion when choosing footwear and clothing items. - Don't forget about skin protection - use sunscreen even on cloudy days. - Minimize jewelry and other accessories to prevent loss or injury. Conclusion: By following this detailed guide, you'll be well prepared to enjoy your time at the fair without worrying about what you're wearing. Being comfortable and properly dressed lets you focus on creating memorable experiences! Can you tell me of a time that uh you were scared something that scared you? Objects that fly using wings get their lift by taking advantage of Bernoulli's principle. Bernoulli's principle says that as a fluid's velocity increases its pressure decreases. Airplanes and birds have an airfoil shape to each of their wings to produce lift. The airfoil shape produces unequal lengths across the top and bottom of the wing. Air splitting at the front of the wing must rejoin at the back of the wing so as not to create a vacuum. Since the top surface is curved upward and is longer it forces the air to move faster across the top than the bottom. Faster moving fluids create less pressure, so the bottom of the wing creates greater pressure producing lift. Racing cars use this principle to produce better traction. They place an airfoil on the back of the car but with the curved surface on the bottom. This is commonly called a spoiler. With the curved surface underneath, greater pressure is generated on the top forcing the back of the car down onto the road. There are four major forces involved in 2. Weight (gravity) In order to fly an object must be able to generate enough lift to overcome its weight. To accomplish this, air must be moving across the wing. This can be done by pushing the wing through the air such as with a jet engine or by moving the air acroos the wing such as with wind. The opposing force of ligt is gravity. Weight is the force of gravity. It is a continous downward force that flying objects must constantly overcome to stay aloft. Objects can glide for a very long period of time but will eventually fall back to earth if they are don't generate enough lift to overcome gravity. The opposing forve of gravity is lift. Thrust is the force that moves the object forward. This is generated by a jet engine, propellar or the backward push of a bird wing. To move forward the flying object must overcome drag. To help reduce drag, airplanes are streamlined and most bird shapes have evolved to be streamlined. The opposing force to thrust is drag. Drag is actually friction between the moving object and the air. It is also referred to as air resistence. The more streamlined, or aerodynamic, an object is the less air resistennce the object generates. The opposite force of drag is thrust. What do you think if you dig? [takes all the objects off the base except for the ball bearing] Title: Auto Sync Fluval Aquarium Lights After a Power Outage Introduction: A power outage can disrupt the schedule of your aquarium lights, which is essential for maintaining a healthy environment for your fish and plants. This tutorial will guide you through setting up an automatic sync system for your Fluval aquarium lights after a power outage, ensuring consistent light cycles regardless of unexpected power interruptions. By following these steps, you'll be able to provide optimal growing conditions while minimizing manual adjustments. **Important Note:** Before starting this process, make sure that all electrical connections are securely fastened and safely connected according to manufacturer specifications. If unsure about any part of the procedure or if there are concerns regarding safety, consult with a professional electrician or contact Fluval customer support. Step 1: Gather Required Materials To auto-sync Fluval aquarium lights after a power outage, you need: * A Fluval LED aquarium lighting fixture (e.g., Fluval Plant 3.0) * An external timer or smart plug compatible with your lighting fixture * Appropriate wiring connectors for securing the connections between devices Step 2: Connect Your Lighting Fixture to the Timer/Smart Plug Connect the power cord of the Fluval LED lighting fixture to the appropriate port on the timer/smart plug. Make sure to match polarity correctly - usually marked as 'L' for live wire and 'N' for neutral wire. Secure the connection using approved wiring connectors to prevent loose contacts or accidental disconnections. Step 3: Configure the External Timer/Smart Plug Settings Set up the timer/smart plug based on your preferred daylight hours for the aquarium. Most tropical freshwater setups benefit from around 8-10 hours of light per day, while planted tanks may require longer periods of illumination due to photosynthetic needs. Adjust the start and end times accordingly, considering local sunrise and sunset schedules if desirable. Step 4: Test the Setup Manually Plug the timer into a wall socket and turn it on. Observe whether the Fluval LED lighting fixture turns on at the scheduled time. Check both morning and evening transitions to ensure proper functioning during both phases of operation. Allow the full cycle to complete before proceeding to the next step. Step 5: Program the Memory Function on the Timer/Smart Plug Some timers have a memory function designed to retain settings even after a power loss. Consult the user manual provided by the manufacturer to activate this feature. Once enabled, the timer should automatically revert to its last configured state once power is restored following an outage. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Always double-check all connections for security and correct polarity before testing or operating the setup. * Regularly inspect the condition of cords, plugs, and other components for signs of wear or damage. Replace them immediately if necessary. * Consider investing in high-quality surge protectors to safeguard electronic equipment against potential voltage spikes caused by storms or utility company issues. * Never attempt to modify or alter the internal circuitry of any device unless explicitly instructed by the manufacturer or authorized personnel. Conclusion: By following these steps and adhering to the recommended practices outlined above, you can successfully implement an automated synchronization system for your Fluval aquarium lights, ensuring continuous operation throughout various power events. As always, prioritize safety when working with electricity and seek professional assistance whenever needed to maintain a thriving aquatic ecosystem. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/Interviews/24/6454MB.cha = = = [Nina goes over to pick up some pictures of herself from the bookcase] [Nina has picked up a small cloth doll with a baby sewn onto her arm. Title: How to Change Your Phone Number on iMessage Introduction: iMessage is a popular instant messaging service developed by Apple that allows you to send texts, documents, photos, videos, and other files over Wi-Fi, iPhone cellular data networks, or any Internet connection available. If you have changed your phone number recently or want to switch to a different one, this tutorial will guide you through changing your phone number on iMessage. Follow these steps carefully to ensure a smooth transition without losing any important conversations or contacts. Step 1: Open Settings App On your iOS device's home screen, find and tap on the gray gear icon labeled 'Settings'. This app contains all the settings and configurations for your device. Step 2: Access Messages Settings In the Settings menu, scroll down until you see 'Messages', which should be located towards the middle of the list. Tap on it to open the iMessage settings. Step 3: Sign Out of Apple ID (Optional) If you are already signed into your Apple ID under 'Send & Receive', sign out before proceeding by tapping on your email address and selecting 'Sign Out' from the popup menu. This step ensures that there won't be any conflicts when updating your phone number associated with your Apple ID. However, if you don't see an active Apple ID here, skip directly to Step 4. Step 4: Enable iMessage Make sure iMessage is toggled ON at the top of the screen; its slider should be green. Keep in mind that disabling iMessage would mean turning off the feature entirely, so only do this if you intend to stop using iMessage altogether. Step 5: Check Current Phone Numbers Under 'You Can Be Reached By iMessage At', check whether your old phone number(s) are listed. To remove them, simply tap on the Edit button at the upper right corner, then hit the red minus (-) button next to the number(s) you wish to delete. Confirm removal by tapping 'Delete'. Step 6: Add New Phone Number After removing unwanted numbers, tap on 'Add Another Email...' below the remaining entries. A new field will appear where you can input your new phone number. Remember to include the country code (+[Country Code][Phone Number]) format. Once entered correctly, click 'Next'. Step 7: Verify New Phone Number Apple will send a verification code via SMS to your newly added phone number. Retrieve this code from your phone and enter it into the provided text box within the given time limit. After successful verification, tap 'Done'. Step 8: Update Contact Information (Recommended) To avoid confusion among your contacts, update your contact information manually in their devices or request them to do so. While they may still receive messages sent to your previous number initially, having the correct info saves future complications. Conclusion: Changing your phone number on iMessage involves signing out of your current Apple ID, enabling iMessage, checking existing connected phone numbers, adding your new number, verifying it, and finally updating your contact details. Following these steps meticulously guarantees a seamless changeover process while preserving ongoing communications and connections. Extracting a .tgz file, also known as a tarball, in Windows using the command line is a straightforward process that can be accomplished with minimal effort once you understand the steps involved. This method utilizes the built-in capabilities of PowerShell or Command Prompt along with tools like 7-Zip or WinRAR. Here's an in-depth guide on extracting a .tgz file in Windows via the command line: **Step 1: Install 7-Zip or WinRAR** Firstly, ensure you have either 7-Zip or WinRAR installed on your system. These are powerful compression tools capable of handling various archive formats including .tgz files. You can download them from their respective official websites - <https://www.7-zip.org/> or <https://www.rarlab.com/>. After installation, proceed to Step 2. **Step 2: Open PowerShell or Command Prompt** Press `Win + X` keys together and select 'Windows Terminal (Admin)' if available; otherwise choose 'Command Prompt (Admin)'. Alternatively, search for "PowerShell" or "cmd" in the Start menu, right-click the result, and then click 'Run as administrator'. Running these applications with administrative privileges ensures proper access rights during extraction. **Step 3: Navigate to the Directory Containing the .tgz File** Use the `cd` (change directory) command followed by the path where your .tgz file resides. For example, if your .tgz file is located at C:\Users\YourName\Downloads\, type `cd C:\Users\YourName\Downloads\`. Press Enter to navigate into the specified folder. Remember to replace "YourName" with your actual username. Key Tip: To move up one level, simply enter `cd ..` and hit Enter. If you want to quickly jump to another drive, just type its letter followed by a colon, e.g., `D:` , then press Enter. **Step 4: Verify the Presence of the .tgz File** List all contents within the current directory by typing `dir` (for Command Prompt) or `ls` (for PowerShell), then press Enter. Check whether your .tgz file appears in the list. Its name should end with ".tgz". **Step 5: Extract the .tgz File Using 7-Zip or WinRAR** Now comes the crucial part - executing the appropriate command to extract the .tgz file. We will employ 7-Zip's `7z.exe` utility here, but similar procedures apply for WinRAR's `rar.exe`. Type the following command, replacing "yourfile.tgz" with the exact filename: For they say that there shall come a stranger who shall be my son-in-law, and that from his loins shall come forth those who shall raise our name even unto the stars. Ollie the Ostrich and Oliver the Octopus By: Victoria Barron Rationale: Children must understand that every letter has a sound associated with it. In order to read, children must blend words beginning with the vowel sound, then the body and coda. In this lesson , students will be learning short o. They will learn ways to remember that o=/o/, then practice by reading a decodable book. Six boxes per student for letterbox lesson Picture of the letter O with octopus legs coming out of it Poster with "Ollie the Ostrich and Oliver the Octopus" Decodable book Doc in the Fog for each student Cards with phoneme awareness words printed: top/tip, fog/fig, lock/land, frog/free, doc/duck Copies of worksheets for each student (url below) 1. Introduce the letter O by asking students what your mouth looks like when you say /o/. Let students make the sound. 2. Have all of the students say /o/ and feel the shape of their mouth as they say the sound. Ask, "What words have this letter in it?" Have the students feel their mouths as they say the word "top". 3. Show the students the picture of the octopus with the tongue tickler written on it. Read it aloud at a regular pace first, then slowly. The third time exaggerate the /o/ sound on each word. O-o-o-ollie the O-o-o-strich and O-o-o-liver the O-o-o-ctopus. Have all of the students say it with you. Repeat. 4. Say, Now we are going to work on recognizing the letter /o/ on words. I am going to say two words and I want you to tell me which word you hear the letter o in. Do you hear /o/ in top or tip, fog or fig, land or lock, frog or free, dock or duck? Great job! 5. Say, Since everyone did such a great job at recognizing which words have the /o/ sound we are going to practice spelling some words that have the letter /o/ in them. Everyone get your squares out and place them flat on your desk. Line up all the letter neatly so you can see them. Be sure to only put one sound in each box." Model the word strong on the board. S-t-r-o-n-g. (one phoneme in each box). The students should work individually on spelling the words. [words: 3-rock, top, job 4-frog, smog, block, stop, flop 5-strong] Say, Raise your hand once you think you have the word spelled out so I can check it. Write all the words on the board and have the students say them after they have spelled them all out. 6. Say, We will now read the book Doc in the Fog. In this book, Doc does some magic to turn tops into dolls and dolls into mops! Uh-oh, what happens when Doc messes with the fog? 7. Have the students read in pairs. Each student should read one page then let the other read the next page. Encourage the students to help each other with words. Assessment: Have all the students complete the work sheet by saying the words to you, then coloring them and practicing writing o. King, Michaela. Oscar the Ostrich loves Olive. One way is how the valleys of a stream and its tributaries look on a map. The Bryant watershed's valleys make a tree-like pattern. Each stream valley branches into smaller ones, and these then branch into still smaller ones. This pattern comes from very large cracks hidden in the bedrock lying underneath that direct the flow of water on the Another way of looking at stream valleys is by their shape. At their headwaters, the valleys of Bryant tributaries are V-shaped. The streams cut right down into the soil and rock because they are flowing steeply downward, and so they flow fast. Fast water carries more sediment, which grinds the stream deeper and deeper into the bottom of the V. A V-shaped valley is a younger valley, because it is cutting back into the higher ground where it begins. ("Younger" means hundreds and thousands of years. "Older" means hundreds of thousands, even millions of years!) The Bryant's valley is older where it has already cut pretty far down through the bedrock. There it flows less steeply on its way to join the North Fork. Where it's older, it's broader, and you find the rich, flat bottomlands good for farming. There the stream "meanders," year by year slowly moving back and forth across the valley, one side losing, one side gaining land. There the land floods in high water times. That's why the bottoms are also called "flood plains". You may notice that it is the outside of a curve that eats into the stream bank. The water flows fastest on the outside of the curve. On the inside curve, the slower water drops its sediment, and the land builds up on that bank. That's where you find gravel bars. Then sycamores sprout. In a few years what was stream bottom has become bottomland! For the pronunciation of single sounds, you will need to focus on the sounds your students have difficulty with. This will depend on the language of your students. For example, many foreign learners have problems pronouncing "th", but this is not an issue for Arab learners as they have this sound in their own language. To teach single sounds, you need to demonstrate the pronunciation while the students listen. You can do this yourself or use a cassette. You can also describe to the students how the sound is produced, if this can be done easily. For example, to pronounce "th" you can explain that they need to stick out their tongue slightly. You also need to constrast the individual sound with another similar sound. You could contrast "th" and "f" or "b" and "v" (b and v are a problem for some Spanish speakers). If students can distinguish difficult sounds it's easier for them to produce them. To help recognition of sounds, you can use an exercise called minimal pairs. Prepare two columns of words that vary on just one sound. Label the columns 1 and 2. For example: Column 1: sat bat had mat Column 2: set bet head met To distinguish between /a/ and /e/ You pronounce one word from each pair and the students say whether it is in column one or two. When you think the students are able to distinguish the sounds, you can ask them to repeat after you. I should say women first. Back in the o-o-o-ld days, getting dressed meant fastening buttons or hooks one by one on your jacket and shoes; took forever. Imagine how late you'd be. An early zipper - called the clasp locker - was displayed at the Chicago World's Fair in 1893. It used a sliding pull-tab that pulled together rows of hooks and eyes, kind of like a zipper. But not quite. They were awkward and tended to come apart at the worst times. Fine for mailbags and shoes, but not quite ready for your jeans. A better version, designed around World War I, had tiny metal bars, or teeth, instead of hooks and eyes. It showed up in Navy life vests and Army gear. By the 1930s, people were buying clothing with zippers. Today, zippers are made of plastic, nylon and other materials besides metal. But they still work much like the ones American soldiers wore almost 90 years ago. Zippers use two simple tools, the wedge and the hook, to form a chain of interlocking teeth. When you pull the tab to close a zipper, tiny wedges inside force the two rows of teeth together. Each tooth is hooked so it meshes with the shape of the opposite tooth. The teeth stay locked together until you pull the tab and the wedges push the teeth apart. Zippers got their name in 1925 when the BF Goodrich company used the invention on a new rubber boot and coined the term from the sound they made. Well, time to go. Find Parking on Google Maps on Your iPhone or iPad Google Maps is a powerful tool that can help you navigate your way through unfamiliar cities, find the best routes, and even locate parking spots. This tutorial will guide you through the process of finding parking on Google Maps using an iPhone or iPad. By following these steps, you'll be able to quickly and efficiently locate available parking near your destination. Step 1: Open Google Maps Locate the Google Maps app on your home screen or within the App Library and tap on its icon to open it. Make sure you have the latest version of the app installed to access all features. If you don't have Google Maps yet, download it from the App Store. Step 2: Search for Your Destination Tap on the search bar at the top of the screen and type in the address or name of your intended location. As you begin typing, Google Maps will provide suggestions based on popular places and previous searches. Select your desired destination from the dropdown list or press 'Search' after entering the full address. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Accurately enter the complete address for better results. - Use voice search if hands-free navigation is more convenient (press the microphone button next to the search bar). Step 3: Set Up Your Route Once your destination appears on the map, tap on the blue direction arrow located just below the search bar. This action will display various transportation modes like driving, walking, cycling, or transit. Choose the most suitable mode according to your preference. After selecting the mode, Google Maps will calculate the optimal route to reach your destination. Step 4: Locating Available Parking After setting up your route, look for the "P" symbol with a number indicating nearby parking options along your chosen path. Tap this symbol to view detailed information about available parking lots and garages, including their addresses, distance from your current location, user ratings, prices, and availability when possible. Some locations may also show real-time occupied spaces vs total capacity. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Consider filtering by price or rating to narrow down choices that suit your needs. - Remember that live availability data might not always be accurate due to constant changes in occupancy levels. Step 5: Reserve a Spot (Optional) If reservable parking is offered in the area, select one of the available options shown in Step 4. You'll then be redirected to the respective third-party website or application where you can proceed with making a reservation and payment if necessary. Step 6: Follow Directions to The Selected Parking Location With the parking spot selected, tap "Directions" to receive turn-by-turn guidance to get there. Once parked, switch back to regular directions to continue navigating toward your final destination. By mastering these simple steps, you'll save time and reduce stress associated with searching for parking, ultimately enhancing your overall experience while exploring new areas or commuting daily. Disable Aero Glass in Windows Vista: A Comprehensive Guide Windows Vista, released by Microsoft in January 2007, introduced several new features and improvements over its predecessors. One such feature was the Aero Glass theme, which provided a visually appealing translucent interface for windows and taskbar. However, some users might prefer to disable this feature due to performance issues or personal preference. This guide will walk you through the process of disabling Aero Glass in Windows Vista, providing in-depth explanations for each step along with key tips and guidelines. **Step 1: Access Personalization Settings** 1. Click on the Start button (the round button usually located at the bottom left corner of your screen) or press the Windows key on your keyboard. 2. Type 'Personalize' into the search bar and click on the 'Personalization' option that appears in the search results. This action will open the Personalization window, where you can change various visual settings for your system. Disabling Aero Glass is all about customizing your computer's appearance to suit your preferences. By accessing the Personalization settings, you are taking the first step towards achieving this goal. **Step 2: Select a Different Theme** 1. Within the Personalization window, locate the section named 'Theme'. 2. Click on the dropdown menu under 'Theme', and you will see a list of available themes. Scroll down until you find either 'Windows Classic' or any other non-Aero theme. 3. Click on the selected theme to apply it. You may be prompted to confirm the changes; if so, click 'Yes'. Selecting a different theme allows you to replace the Aero Glass theme with an alternative one that does not have transparency effects. The two primary options for disabling Aero Glass are 'Windows Classic' and 'Basic', but third-party themes could also serve this purpose. **Key Tips:** * If you cannot find a suitable non-Aero theme within the default options, consider exploring third-party themes designed specifically for Windows Vista. Websites like DeviantArt offer numerous user-created themes that do not include Aero Glass effects. * Remember that changing your theme will affect not only the desktop background and color scheme but also various interface elements like window borders, icons, and sounds. Be prepared for these alterations when selecting a new theme. **Step 3: Verify Aero Glass has been Disabled** 1. After applying the new theme, minimize all active application windows. 2. Observe the taskbar and window borders. If they no longer possess transparent qualities, then Aero Glass has successfully been disabled. Verifying the success of disabling Aero Glass ensures that your system now runs without the resource-intensive translucency effects. This step guarantees that you have achieved the desired outcome. **Guidelines:** * Keep in mind that disabling Aero Glass might improve overall system performance, particularly on older hardware. However, this improvement may be minimal depending on your specific configuration. * Some applications may behave differently after disabling Aero Glass. Test essential software thoroughly to ensure compatibility with your chosen theme. In conclusion, disabling Aero Glass in Windows Vista offers both aesthetic and potential performance benefits. By following this comprehensive guide, you can confidently navigate the process while understanding each step's significance. With careful consideration given to key tips and guidelines, you will enjoy a smoother and more tailored computing experience. There's nothing harder about the red one. Title: How to Contact Hermes Parcel Delivery: A Comprehensive Guide Hermes is one of the leading parcel delivery services in Europe, delivering over 400 million parcels every year. If you need assistance with your package or have questions about their service, this guide will help you learn how to contact Hermes quickly and effectively. We've broken down the process into eight easy-to-follow steps, complete with helpful tips and guidelines along the way. ### Step 1: Identify Your Reason for Contacting Hermes Before reaching out to Hermes, determine the purpose of your communication. This can include: * Tracking a parcel * Filing a complaint or claim * Requesting redelivery * Changing delivery preferences * Finding nearby drop-off points * General inquiries Knowing why you want to contact them ensures that you choose the appropriate method of communication and prepare any necessary information beforehand. Key tip: Keep your tracking number(s) handy if you are following up on a specific shipment. ### Step 2: Choose Your Communication Method There are multiple ways to get in touch with Hermes based on your needs and urgency: #### Online Support Center Visit <https://www.myhermes.co.uk/contact-us.html> to access Hermes' online support center. Here, you can find answers to frequently asked questions (FAQ), track your parcels, request redeliveries, change delivery options, and more. Benefits: The online support center offers quick solutions without waiting times. It also has extensive resources available 24/7. #### Live Chat Access live chat by clicking 'Chat Now' at the bottom right corner of the page when visiting <https://www.myhermes.co.uk/> during business hours (Monday - Friday, 8 am - 6 pm; Saturday, 8 am - 5 pm). Benefits: Live chat allows real-time interaction with customer support agents who can assist with immediate concerns. However, note that wait times may vary depending on demand. #### Email You can email Hermes using the form provided on their website under 'Get in Touch.' Fill out the required fields, including name, address, phone number, subject, and message. Alternatively, send an email directly to hermessupport@hermesworld.com. Benefits: Emailing provides written documentation of interactions, making it suitable for complex issues requiring thorough explanation or evidence submission. Expect responses within 24-48 hours. #### Phone Call Call Hermes Customer Services at 0330 808 54 [talks to MOT about what will happen next] Can't come in later and say you're hungry. [makes another circular movement with her hand toward the paper] Prince (1968) found virtually no depression in most Asian and African countries. However, psychologists such as Zhang and Kleinman have found disorders with similar symptoms, but different names. For example, in the 1980s, Zhang found that in China, there was little mention of depression. However, instead, a disorder called Neurasthenia was far more common. When interviewing Neurasthenia patients, Zhang found that their symptoms matched the symptoms of depression by over 80%. While the term "depression" was practically unheard of in China, Neurasthenia could well have been a similar disorder - or the same thing. The original group included Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Jeremy Spencer and Bob Brunning. In 1968 Danny Kirwan joined the band. He was then an 18-year-old guitarist and singer. Peter Green left and, in 1971 was replaced by Christine and John McVie plus Bob Welch. When Welch left he was replaced by Americans Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks. You had cavities, and you can't have anymore. Radical equations are equations that have square root terms. When solving radical equations, try to isolate the radical expression on one side of the equation and then square both sides (it is the inverse operation of taking a square root). If there are two or more terms on the opposite side of the equation, remember to draw parenthesis around that expression before squaring. You guys are going to be working on solving radical equations. What that means is that you're going to have a square root and an equal sign. So I'm just going to tell you just one sentence that's going to help you know how to do these. What you need to do is isolate the square root sign and then square both sides of the equals. If you can remember that, and if that makes sense in your head, you'll be able to do these really well. Let me tell you one more time. Title: How to Call Sweden from the United States Introduction: Making international calls can sometimes be confusing due to different country codes, area codes, and calling procedures. This tutorial will guide you through the process of calling Sweden from the United States with clear and detailed steps. By following this guide, you'll be able to make successful calls to Sweden while avoiding potential issues such as misdialing or high charges. Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. Familiarize yourself with the necessary codes: To call any international location, you need to know three essential codes - the exit code of your country (in this case, 011 for the US), the country code of the destination (46 for Sweden), and the local area code and phone number. 2. Check rates and plans: Before making an international call, check with your telephone service provider about their rates and available plans for calling Sweden. Some providers offer discounted international calling packages that might save you money compared to regular per-minute charges. 3. Dial accurately: When entering numbers, ensure there are no mistakes, especially when dealing with long sequences of digits during international calls. A single error could lead to unsuccessful connections or unexpected costs. 4. Use calling cards or apps: If your current plan has expensive international calling rates, consider using prepaid calling cards specifically designed for international calls or VoIP applications like Skype, which may provide more cost-effective alternatives. 5. Keep time differences in mind: Be aware of the time difference between the two countries before placing your call. You don't want to wake someone up in the middle of the night accidentally! Step-by-step Instructions: 1. Begin by dialing the US exit code (011). The purpose of this code is to signal that you're making an international call. It allows your carrier to route your call correctly to its intended recipient outside the U.S. 2. Next, enter Sweden's country code (+46). Every nation has its unique country code used to identify itself in global telecommunications networks. For Sweden, it consists of four digits - 'plus,' followed by 'four six.' Note that some phones allow direct inputting of the '+' symbol; otherwise, replace it with the corresponding international access code (011) from Step 1. 3. Afterward, dial the Swedish local area code (*without* initial zeros if present*). Local area codes vary depending on the specific region within Sweden you wish to reach. They usually range from one to four digits long. Unlike many North American regions where NANP rules eliminate leading zeroes, certain Swedish area codes retain these prefixes (e.g., 08 for Stockholm); hence exclude them only when explicitly instructed. 4. Finally, input the recipient's local phone number. Once connected to the correct regional network via the appropriate area code, dial the individual's seven-digit phone number. Double-check all entered figures to avoid errors. Summary Example: To recap, assuming you intend to call a friend residing in Stockholm whose number starts with '08' followed by '7xx xx xx', here's what you should dial: (011) + (46) + (8) + (7xx xx xx) OR +46 8 7xx xx xx Conclusion: By understanding the required codes and diligently following our outlined steps, making international calls from the US to Sweden becomes straightforward and manageable. Always remember to verify rates, double-check entries, and respect time zone differences to optimize communication efficiency and maintain goodwill among transcontinental acquaintances. Fog forms when a layer of warm, wet air forms close to the ground and another layer of cooler, drier air forms on top of the warmer layer. When the ground begins to cool down, the water droplets in the warm, wet air begin to condense to form fog. This type of fog is called radiation fog. - Two soda bottles with necks about the size of an ice cube - Two ice cubes about the size of the soda bottle necks - Hot water - Cold water - Journal or logbook Estimated Experiment Time A few hours at most. - 1. Empty the soda bottles and rinse them out thoroughly. Label each soda bottle, one with "Cold water" and the other with "Hot water." - 2. Fill the first soda bottle of the way full with cold water. Place an ice cube in the neck of the soda bottle so it is wedged in but does not pass all the way through the bottle. What happens? Record your findings in your journal. - 3. Repeat step two with the second soda bottle, only this time, filling the bottle of the way full with hot water. Place the ice cube in the neck of this soda bottle and note what happens. This experiment is an accurate simulation of radiation fog. Watch what happens with each soda bottle and remember to record your findings in your journal or logbook. Fog will form in one of the bottles - make a prediction based on the description of how fog forms on which bottle will produce a simulation of fog. Which one do you think will produce the fog? Fog will form in the bottle with the hot water, but it will not form in the bottle of cold water. Why do you think that is? How does the description of how fog is formed relate to your findings? What do you think would happen if you repeated this experiment with several soda bottles with different temperatures of water? What do you think would happen if you used only warm water (not hot) or lukewarm (almost cool) water? Do you think more or less fog would form in the soda bottle? How do the results of the experiment apply to real life situations? "Encyclop dia Britannica Online" is a web site with more than 120,000 articles and is updated often. It has features, updates and links to news reports from "The New York Times" and the BBC every day. People need to pay to use the website. Special discounts are given to schools, colleges and libraries since these big groups of people are important in Britannica's business. Articles can be read online for free, but only the first few sentences can be seen. Beginning in early 2007, the "Britannica" let people read the articles for free if they are linked to another website, since these links let the articles appear more often and easily in search engines. A mountain is a large natural rise of the Earth's surface that usually has a "summit" (the name for a mountain's top, which can also be called a peak). It is usually steeper and taller than a hill. Mountains are often thought of as being a hill which is larger than 600 metres (about 2,000 feet). However, some definitions say a mountain is a hill larger than 300 meters (about 1,000 feet). Women actors sometimes act the roles of young boys, because in some ways a woman is more similar to a boy than a man is. For example, a woman usually plays the role of Peter Pan. In pantomime, a sort of play for children (not the same as mime), the most important young man is also a woman. Opera has some "pants roles" which women traditionally sing. These women are usually mezzo-sopranos, which means they sing with a voice that is high but not very high. Examples are Hansel in "", and Cherubino in "The Marriage of Figaro". Step Two Trace an outline of her hands on the two remaining plates. Step Three Cut out the trace. This will be used for the ears. Step Four Trace an an outline(lengthwise) of a toilet paper roll onto grey construction paper and cut it out. She can also use white paper and paint it grey. This will be the elephant's trunk. Step Five Next, glue one hand cutout to each side of the paper plate for the elephant's ears. Step Six Then glue the the trunk to the middle of the plate. Step Seven Cut out some eyes and a mouth from construction paper and glue them on. Step Eight Align the face of the elephant on top of the remaining colored plate so that they overlap and glue it in place. Step Nine Cut out a tail and legs and glue them to the rest of the body. Downloading YouTube videos can be a convenient way to watch your favorite content offline or on a device without internet access. However, it's important to note that downloading videos from YouTube may infringe on the copyright of the video owner, so always make sure you have permission to do so before proceeding. With that said, here is our detailed guide on how to download YouTube videos easily: 1. **Find a reliable YouTube video downloader.** There are many YouTube video downloaders available online, but not all of them are safe or reliable. Some may contain malware or viruses that could harm your computer. To avoid this, stick with well-known and reputable downloaders such as 4K Video Downloader, SaveFromNet, or KeepVid. These tools have been around for a while and have built up a good reputation among users. 2. **Copy the YouTube video URL.** Once you've found the video you want to download, copy its URL from the address bar at the top of your web browser. Make sure you copy the entire link, including the "https://" part. This will ensure that the downloader can find the correct video. 3. **Paste the URL into the downloader.** Open the YouTube video downloader you chose in Step 1 and paste the URL into the designated field. The downloader should automatically detect the video and give you options for the quality and format of the download. 4. **Choose the video quality and format.** Depending on the downloader, you may have several options for the quality and format of the video. Higher quality videos will take up more space on your device, but they will also look better when played back. Common formats include MP4, MKV, and AVI. Choose the one that best suits your needs. 5. **Start the download.** After selecting the quality and format, click the "Download" button to start the process. Depending on the size of the video and your internet connection speed, the download may take some time to complete. Be patient and let the downloader do its job. 6. **Locate the downloaded video file.** Once the download is finished, locate the video file on your device. It should be saved in the default download location specified by your web browser, but you can check the settings of the downloader to confirm. 7. **Play the downloaded video.** Finally, double-click the video file to play it back on your device. If you encounter any issues playing the video, try using a different media player or converting the file to a different format using a tool like Handbrake. **Key Tips:** * Always make sure you have permission to download a YouTube video before doing so. * Use a reputable and reliable YouTube video downloader to avoid potential security risks. * Consider the video quality and format carefully before starting the download. * Check the download location and convert the file if necessary to play it back smoothly. **Guidelines:** * Only download YouTube videos for personal use and with proper authorization. * Avoid sharing downloaded videos without permission from the original creator. * Respect the rights of video owners and adhere to YouTube's terms of service. Once upon a time, two good friends named Jack and Jill lived together. They liked to do lots of fun things together. One day, they went to the park together. Title: How to Deal with a Difficult Parent Introduction: Dealing with a difficult parent can be challenging and emotionally draining. However, understanding their perspective, setting boundaries, communicating effectively, and seeking support can help improve your relationship. This guide will provide you with in-depth steps and practical advice on dealing with a tough parent while maintaining your well-being. Step 1: Understand Their Perspective Difficult parents often act out due to stress, fear, or unresolved issues from their past. By putting yourself in their shoes, you can better understand their behavior and respond more empathetically. * Key Tip: Avoid taking their actions personally; instead, try to identify any underlying causes for their behavior. Step 2: Set Healthy Boundaries Establishing clear limits is crucial when dealing with a demanding or controlling parent. Make sure these boundaries are assertive but respectful. * Guideline: Clearly communicate what behaviors are acceptable and which ones aren't. Be consistent in enforcing them, yet open to negotiation if necessary. Step 3: Choose Your Battles Wisely Not every issue requires an immediate confrontation. Assess the situation and determine whether addressing the concern is worth the potential conflict. * Key Tip: Focus on major concerns that significantly impact your life or well-being rather than minor annoyances. Step 4: Communicate Effectively Clear, concise communication is essential when interacting with a difficult parent. Use "I" statements to express your feelings without blaming or criticizing them. * Guideline: Listen actively by summarizing their points and asking clarifying questions before responding. This approach shows respect and encourages mutual understanding. Step 5: Stay Calm During Conflicts Maintaining composure during disagreements prevents escalating tensions and fosters productive conversations. Take deep breaths, count to ten, or remove yourself from the situation temporarily if needed. * Key Tip: Practice mindfulness techniques to enhance emotional regulation and reduce reactivity in high-stress situations. Step 6: Seek Outside Support Connecting with friends, family members, therapists, or support groups can offer valuable insight, guidance, and encouragement as you navigate this challenge. * Guideline: Share your experiences openly and honestly, seeking constructive feedback and alternative perspectives. Step 7: Encourage Professional Help If your parent struggles with mental health issues, addiction, or other personal challenges, suggest they seek professional assistance. Offer to research options together or accompany them to appointments. * Key Tip: Remember that ultimately, individuals must choose to accept help and work towards change themselves. Step 8: Prioritize Self-Care Taking care of your physical, emotional, and psychological needs is vital when managing relationships with difficult parents. Engage in regular exercise, eat nutritious meals, practice relaxation techniques, and maintain social connections. * Guideline: Establish routines that promote self-care and resilience, enabling you to manage stressors more effectively. Conclusion: Dealing with a tough parent involves patience, understanding, and determination. By following these steps and utilizing key tips and guidelines provided throughout this tutorial, you can foster a healthier dynamic between you and your parent while preserving your own well-being. They have been bred by humans. The earliest known fossil of a domestic dog is from 31,700 years ago in Belgium. Dogs have lived with people for at least 30,000 years. In 2013, a study was published that showed that the skull and teeth of a canid, dated to 33,000 years ago, had characteristics closer to a dog than to a wolf, and the authors conclude that "this specimen may represent a dog in the very early stages of domestication, i.e. an "incipient" dog." The researchers go on to suggest that it was, however, a line that did not lead to modern dogs. Genetically, this material is closer to that of a modern dog than to that of a wolf. Other signs of domestication are that sometimes, dogs were buried together with humans. Evidence of this is a tomb in Bonn, where a man of about 50 years of age, a woman of about 25 years of age, the remains of a dog, plus other artifacts were found. At one point, Montana was liberal in federal elections and conservative in state elections. Today, the opposite is true. Although Montana is very conservative in presidential elections (it has not voted for a Democrat since 1964), it is more liberal than most states in the upper Rocky Mountains region. As one example of this, Montana is represented by one Democrat and one Republican in the Senate. There are no Democratic senators from Utah, Idaho, Wyoming, North Dakota or South Dakota. The Montana House of Representatives, which has 100 members, includes 58 Republicans and 42 Democrats, making it more liberal than any state legislature in Wyoming or Idaho. Players score a "try" if they manage to touch the ball to the ground on or beyond the other team's in-goal. After a try, the team that scored the try then has an opportunity to kick a goal. This is known as a "conversion kick". A goal that is scored from a conversion kick is worth two points in addition to the points of a try. A try is worth five points in rugby union, and four points in rugby league. After a team scores points, play restarts with the non-scoring team kicking the ball from the halfway line toward the opposing team's field goal. Earthquakes by Claire and Nisha We're Claire and Nisha, and we live in a place where earthquakes are NOT science fiction! We live in San Francisco, where you can really feel the earth move under your feet. The last big quake here was in 1989, before we were born. We wondered if we could learn about how the earth moves in an earthquake by looking around the city. Our question: How does the earth move when there's an earthquake? What did we do? We visited the Lawrence Hall of Science to learn about how earthquakes happen. We saw that the San Francisco area sits right on top of the boundary between two "plates" below the surface of the ground. We found an earthquake simulation we could stand on, that shows how the plates either move up and down, or move past each other. As we went around town, we looked for cracks in the streets, buildings, or ground that resembled these models. What did we find out? We found lots of evidence of earthquake activity where the ground moved past each other. The curbs in the neighborhoods near Berkeley had shifted by as much as 18 inches! And at Berkeley Stadium, the walls of the stadium have cracked, and the two pieces have shifted by 6 inches or more. Some of these shifts have happened slowly over the years. This is called "fault creep". Other times, the shift happens all at once, and that's when there's a serious earthquake. - Make your own earthquake detector, or "seismograph". Go to http://cse.ssl.berkeley.edu/lessons/indiv/davis/hs/Seismograph.html to see how. Use it to detect tremors in your house, such as when you jump on the floor. Can your seismograph detect your feet stomping? Take the seismograph outside, too, and see if you can make the ground vibrate enough by jumping to make the seismograph pen wiggle around. - Build a model town on a platform out of blocks, cardboard, clay or other materials, and simulate an earthquake by slowly sliding the model platform back and forth a few times. Which parts of the model survive the earthquake? Make different sizes and shapes of "buildings" to see which are the most durable in an earthquake. - Use this earth science investigation as a science fair project idea for your elementary or middle school science fair! Then tell us about it! " "I hope," said William, "that things may yet be accommodated; but do not take any resolution, let us act as occasions arise. The old servant, Joseph, at some distance, with his head leaning forward, as listening with the utmost attention to what passed. There are no bridges across the entire width of the river. This is not because the river would be too wide to bridge; for most of its length, engineers could build a bridge across the river easily. For most of its course, the river flows through the Amazon Rainforest, where there are very few roads and cities. Most of the time, the crossing can be done by a ferry, so there is no need to build a bridge. The Manaus Iranduba Bridge linking the cities of Manaus and Iranduba spans the Rio Negro (a tributary of the Amazon). It tells you that there are 2 sets of tens in the tens place. 10+10 = 20 The last or right digit is the ones place. Politics in Mexico became violent and several Mexican States declared independence. On March 2, 1836, Texas declared independence from Mexico and soon they were at war. Texas won, but Mexico refused to recognize Texas as an independent sovereign Republic. Texas soon requested entry to the United States. Years later, in 1845, the US added Texas as a state, and Mexico broke off diplomatic relations. Mexico resisted offers by the U.S. to buy land extending from Texas to the Pacific Ocean. In 1846, a dispute over the border between Texas and Mexico resulted in armed conflict, and the Mexican-American War began. The United States won the war, and by the Treaty of Guadalupe-Hidalgo, in 1848 won lands that later formed the states of California, Arizona, Nevada, Utah, New Mexico, southwestern Colorado, and southwestern Wyoming. Mexico received 15 million dollars and gave up its claims to Texas. [Maisie flips pages to look at back of book] Title: Get Soft Lips Overnight ============================== Are you tired of having chapped, dry lips? This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to get soft lips overnight. By following these steps, you'll wake up with smooth, supple lips that are ready for your favorite lipstick or gloss. ### Step 1: Exfoliate Your Lips Exfoliating removes dead skin cells from the surface of your lips, revealing softer skin underneath. Here's how to do it: 1. **Create a Lip Scrub:** Mix together brown sugar and honey to create a gentle lip scrub. The brown sugar acts as an exfoliant, while the honey provides moisture. You can also add a drop of olive oil for added hydration. 2. **Apply the Scrub:** Gently rub the mixture onto your lips using circular motions. Be careful not to be too harsh, as this could irritate your skin. Focus on any rough patches or areas where your lips feel particularly dry. 3. **Rinse Off:** After one minute, rinse off the scrub with warm water. Pat your lips dry with a clean towel. Key Tips: - Don't over-exfoliate! Once or twice a week is sufficient; more frequent exfoliation may lead to redness and inflammation. - Avoid licking your lips after exfoliating, as saliva can further dry out your lips. ### Step 2: Hydrate Your Lips Hydration is crucial for maintaining soft, plump lips. Follow these steps to keep your lips moisturized throughout the night: 1. **Choose a Heavy Duty Lip Balm:** Opt for a thicker balm containing ingredients like shea butter, cocoa butter, or petroleum jelly. These substances form a protective barrier around your lips, sealing in moisture. 2. **Apply Generously:** Coat your entire lip area - including the corners of your mouth - with a generous layer of balm. Make sure there are no dry spots left uncovered. 3. **Reapply Before Bedtime:** Just before going to sleep, reapply the balm. This ensures maximum hydration while you snooze. Key Guidelines: - Look for fragrance-free and dye-free products, as artificial ingredients can cause irritation. - Consider applying a humidifier in your bedroom at night to prevent dry air from dehydrating your lips. ### Step 3: Protect Your Lips During the Day Proper care during daylight hours contributes to overall lip health and prevents future damage. Keep these suggestions in mind: 1. **Stay Hydrated:** Drink plenty of water throughout the day to maintain proper hydration levels within your body. 2. **Avoid Licking Your Lips:** Saliva evaporates quickly, leaving your lips even drier than before. 3. **Wear Sunscreen:** Apply a lip balm with SPF when spending time outside to protect against harmful UV rays. 4. **Limit Matte Lipsticks:** While matte shades look great, they tend to be drying. Try using a lip primer or balm beneath your lipstick to minimize direct contact between the product and your lips. By following these steps, you'll have soft, kissable lips by morning. Remember, consistency is key when caring for your lips, so make these habits part of your daily routine. The owl got out and flew away. Soon piggies heard a key turning in her door. Title: How to Diagnose Anorexia: A Comprehensive Guide Anorexia nervosa is a serious mental health disorder characterized by restricted food intake, an intense fear of gaining weight, and a distorted body image. If you suspect that someone you know may have anorexia, it's essential to seek professional help immediately. This guide will provide a step-by-step process to diagnose anorexia, but it's crucial to remember that only medical professionals can make a definitive diagnosis. Here are some guidelines to help you along the way: 1. Learn about the signs and symptoms of anorexia: Before attempting to diagnose anyone, familiarize yourself with the common warning signs and symptoms of anorexia. These include: * Restricting food intake or following overly strict diets * Intense fear of gaining weight or becoming fat, even when underweight * Distorted self-image regarding body shape and weight * Excessive exercise routine * Denial of hunger or feeling full quickly after eating * Social withdrawal, irritability, or depression * Obsession with calories, grams of fat, or nutrition facts * Evidence of purging behaviors (vomiting, using laxatives) 2. Encourage seeking professional help: If you observe these signs and symptoms in someone close to you, gently encourage them to consult a healthcare provider. Explain your concerns without judgment and offer support during their journey towards recovery. It's important to emphasize that seeking help is a sign of strength rather than weakness. 3. Rule out physical causes: Consult a physician first to rule out any underlying medical conditions that could be causing significant weight loss. Examples include hyperthyroidism, diabetes, gastrointestinal issues, or cancer. Once physical causes have been ruled out, consider possible psychological factors. 4. Identify potential risk factors: Several factors increase the likelihood of developing anorexia. Familiarizing yourself with these risks might aid in understanding whether someone has this condition. Risk factors include: * Genetic predisposition * Family history of eating disorders, anxiety, or depression * Personality traits such as perfectionism, obsessiveness, or neuroticism And do you regularly have um soup XXX? While he wasn't looking he poured too much milk in his cereal bowl. Title: How to Perform a Boneless Flip Performing a boneless flip is an impressive trick that requires practice and precision. This move involves lifting your body off the skateboard while you're in mid-air, flipping the board 180 degrees, then landing back on it smoothly. Here's our comprehensive guide to help you master this technique. **Step 1: Get Familiar with Your Skateboard** Before attempting any tricks, ensure you are comfortable riding your skateboard regularly. Practice balancing, steering, stopping, and starting until these basic skills become second nature. **Step 2: Master the Pop Shove-it** The pop shove-it is a fundamental precursor skill for the boneless flip. It involves jumping into the air while using your rear foot to pop the tail of the skateboard and spinning it 180 degrees horizontally before reattaching your feet in mid-air and landing safely. Spend time perfecting this maneuver as it forms the basis of the boneless flip. **Key Tip**: Focus on getting enough height from your pop so that you have ample time to execute the rest of the trick. **Step 3: Learn the Manual** A manual is where one leg (usually the front) stays on the ground while the other lifts the board up onto its back wheels. Practicing manuals will aid in learning how to balance without having both feet planted firmly on the deck. **Key Guideline**: Start by practicing static manuals at slow speeds, gradually increasing speed once control improves. **Step 4: Combine Manual with Pop Shove-it** Now try combining steps two and three - perform a pop shove-it but instead of jumping straight back onto the board, hold the manual briefly during the spin. This trains your muscles and mind to separate these actions, preparing you for the full boneless flip. **Step 5: Perfect the Boneless Grab** This move entails grabbing the edge of the skateboard between your legs after popping the tail. Bend your knees slightly upon takeoff, reach down, grip the board, then pull it towards your body as you leap. Remember to keep holding onto the board throughout the jump. **Step 6: Add the Board Rotation** Once you've grasped the boneless grab, introduce the rotation component. As soon as you lift the board, twist your wrist quickly to rotate the deck 180 degrees horizontally. Keep practising until you can consistently flip the board around correctly. **Step 7: Release and Land** After achieving consistent rotation, work on releasing the board just before impact. Time this carefully; if done too early or late, you may miss the board entirely. Aim to let go when your feet would naturally realign above the bolts. Lastly, bend your knees upon landing to absorb shock and maintain stability. **Safety Measures:** * Always wear protective gear such as helmets, knee pads, elbow pads, and wrist guards. * Choose appropriate surfaces free from obstructions, debris, or uneven terrain. * Beginners should start small, building up height, distance, and complexity over time. Remember, patience is vital. Don't rush through the stages; rather, focus on refining each element individually before moving onto the next. With dedication and perseverance, you'll be executing flawless boneless flips in no time! Then I'm going to get a rock too. There are two ways to make nuclear weapons: fission weapons (also called atomic bombs or A-Bombs) and fusion weapons (also called hydrogen bombs, H-Bombs or thermonuclear weapons). The way they make energy for the nuclear explosion is different. Fusion weapons make bigger explosions. Fission weapons use a special isotope of uranium or plutonium. Fusion weapons use a special isotope of hydrogen. Nuclear weapons have been used twice in war, both times by the United States against Japan near the end of World War II. On August 6, 1945, the U.S. Army Air Forces detonated a uranium gun-type fission bomb nicknamed "Little Boy" over the Japanese city of Hiroshima; three days later, on August 9, the U.S. Army Air Forces detonated a plutonium implosion-type fission bomb nicknamed "Fat Man" over the Japanese city of Nagasaki. These bombings caused injuries that resulted in the deaths of approximately 200,000 civilians and military personnel. The ethics of these bombings and their role in Japan's surrender are subjects of debate. Once upon a time, there was a little boy who lived in a forest. He was a very helpful boy and he loved to whistle. He would whistle while he adventure around the forest, looking for things to help his animal friends. One day, while he was whistling and looking around, he heard a strange sound. He followed the sound and he ended up near a river. He saw a fish swimming in the river, but it was struggling to breath. The little boy knew this meant the fish needed oxygen. He quickly ran over to the nearest tree and he started to whistle. When the fish heard the sound, it swam toward the little boy. The boy then used his extra-long leaves from the tree to make a bridge between the bank and the fish, so that it could get to the oxygen it needed. The fish thanked the little boy for his help and swam away, feeling much better. The little boy smiled and resumed his whistling, happy that he had been able to help. [points to ball rolling across the floor] Racing into Fluency Growing Independence and Fluency Growing Independence and Fluency Rationale: For students to be fluent readers they must read quickly, automatically, and expressively. Not only should students read with speed, ease, and expression, but they should also be able to comprehend what they are reading. One way to increase fluency is through repeated readings. Reading a passage of text multiple times allows students to become more fluent readers. In this lesson, students will gain fluency through repeated readings. Materials: One piece of cardstock with the sentence "The hare zoomed along the road to win the race." for each group, a stopwatch for each group, one copy of "The Tortoise and the Hare" for each group, a racing guide with a tortoise and a hare racing on it in which the student wishes to be the hare will be given to each group. The tortoise will be ahead each reading until the desired fluency time is reached (then the race will be won), and one copy of Is Jo Home? Each group will need a calculator. 1. Explain to students what being a fluent reader means. Say "It is very important that we all become fluent readers. To be a fluent reader, you must read with speed and it should be easy to read the words. A fluent reader also understands what they are reading. When we are fluent readers our reading sounds smoother and better. It also lets us enjoy what we are reading because it is easier to read and we understand what we are reading." 2. Demonstrate a fluent reader and a non-fluent reader. Say "To review what it means to be a fluent reader and non-fluent reader I am going to read a page from the book Is Jo Home? I hhhhhhooope Jjjjjoooo is hhhooome. Shhhhee wwwwilll ppplllaaaay wwwithhh mmmeeee. (Read one sentence from the book) (The first time read the sentence very slowly, choppy and without expression.) That is what a beginning reader sounds like. They read very slow, choppy and without expression. Now I am going to read the same page from Is Jo Home? I hope Jo is home. She will play with me. (This time read the text faster, with ease, and with expression.) That is what a fluent reader sounds like. They read the text quickly, smoothly, and with expression. Our goal is for everyone to be a fluent reader." 3. Explain to the students that they are going to be doing repeated readings. Say "To make you a better reader, and a more fluent reader, we are going to do repeated readings. I am going to put you with a partner and you are going to take turns reading. I am going to give each group a sentence on a piece of paper. I want each of you to read the sentence out loud to your partner. The first time you make be a little slow and the words may not flow smoothly, but that's okay because you are going to read the sentence four more times to your partner. By the end, you will be reading the sentence more fluently because you are familiar with the words in the sentence and the words will make more sense." Pass out the piece of cardstock with the sentence on it to each group. "The hare zoomed along the road to win the race." 4. Explain to the students that they are going to be doing timed readings. Say " Now we are going to do a timed reading. Each group is going to be reading the book The Tortoise and the Hare. The tortoise and the hare are always racing one another, and I want you to race yourself to get the best reading you can. The Hare is very quick, but the tortoise is very tricky. You can be whatever character you want to be. You'll have to read the story to find out who will win the race. (Pass to each group a copy of The Tortoise and the Hare, a stopwatch to each group, and the fluency racing board to each group.) While one person is reading, their partner will be timing them. The reader should read as well as they can. If you come to a tricky word, remember to use your cover up critter and do the best you can. The reader will read all the way through the story. When the story is finished you will record the amount of time it took to read the story. Now the partner will have a turn, and the other student will do the same thing the last student did. Using the calculator you will divide the number given (I will multiply the number of words in the book by 60 for them) by seconds it took to read the book. You will put your rabbits and tortoises where they go on the chart in the place that corresponds with the number you came up with. You will repeat this step until you reach the goal, which has a finish line. I will be walking around the room. If you need help just raise your hand." Assessment: After everyone finishes their timed readings and reaches their goals, I will walk around the room and look at everyone's fluency racing chart to see how the student's fluency progressed. I will then take each student individually and do the same reading with The Tortoise and the Hare. This will give me a chance to listen to each student's reading and check for speed, ease, and expression. Adams, Jennifer. Off to the Races! Clabby, Caitlin. [bumps box of toys as he pulls beads apart] Title: How to Change Which Doors Unlock on Your Toyota Prius Hybrid Synergy Drive (HSD) Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through changing which doors unlock on your Toyota Prius HSD. This modification can be useful if you prefer having only the driver's door unlock when pressing the remote button or want all doors to unlock simultaneously. By following these steps, you can customize this feature according to your preference. Note that this procedure applies to most Toyota Prius models with HSD from 2004-2015; however, there might be slight variations depending upon the specific model year and trim level. Materials Needed: * Toyota Techstream diagnostic tool or equivalent OBD-II scan tool compatible with Toyota vehicles * Computer with internet access Step 1: Gain Access to Vehicle Data To change the door lock settings, we need to access the vehicle's data using an appropriate diagnostic tool like the Toyota Techstream. If you don't have one, consider renting it from a local auto parts store or purchasing a compatible OBD-II scanner online. Connect the device to your computer and ensure both are properly connected to the car via the OBD-II port (usually located near the steering column). Step 2: Launch Diagnostic Software Launch the diagnostic software provided with your Techstream unit or any other compatible software installed on your computer. Select "Toyota" as the manufacturer and then choose your specific model and year. Follow the prompts until you reach the main menu. Step 3: Enter Service Mode From the main menu, navigate to the "Vehicle Information" section and look for an option labeled "ECU Reset." Within ECU Reset, find and select "Enter Service mode." Confirm that you wish to enter service mode by entering the required code. Once entered correctly, you should receive confirmation that the vehicle is now in service mode. Step 4: Modify Door Lock Settings Now, return to the main menu and navigate to "Body Electrical Control," followed by "Door Lock System." Here, you will see various options related to the door locks, including our target setting - "Unlock All Doors With One Button Press" or similar wording based on the version of the software you are using. Toggle this setting between enabled and disabled to suit your preferences. Save the changes before exiting the system. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Be sure to read through all available information regarding the chosen setting before making adjustments. Some systems may require additional actions after enabling certain features. * Keep track of any modifications made during servicing modes since they could affect warranty coverage or future troubleshooting efforts. * Always double-check your work once completed. Test out the new door unlock configuration several times to confirm proper functionality. * Remember that improper usage of diagnostic tools or unauthorized tinkering with vehicle settings may lead to issues down the line. Proceed at your own risk. Conclusion: By successfully completing these steps, you have effectively modified the behavior of your Toyota Prius HSD's door locks. Enjoy the added convenience brought about by tailoring the vehicle's functions to better align with your personal needs and habits. Vietnam's history has long been characterized by the neighborhood of China in the north. For about 1,000 years, northern Vietnam belonged to China, but from 938 the country became independent and later expanded southward at the expense of the Champa kingdom. In the 19th century the country was colonized by France and during the Second World War, the country was occupied by Japan. After this war, the colonial empire did not have the resources to restore the regime and lost the military battle against the liberation forces. This led to the division of the country, which in turn led to the Vietnam War with major human and material losses for the country. The war ended on 30 April 1975 by the fact that North Vietnam took the southern part. After experimental planning in the 1970s and 1980s, the economy was reformed in a market economy direction. Photons (from Greek , meaning "light"), in many atomic models in physics, are particles which transmit light. In other words, light is carried over space by photons. Photon is an elementary particle that is its own antiparticle. In quantum mechanics each photon has a characteristic quantum of energy that depends on frequency: A photon associated with light at a higher frequency will have more energy (and be associated with light at a shorter wavelength). Some people who have schizophrenia have differences in their brain structure compared to those who do not have the disorder. These differences are often in the parts of the brain that manage memory, organization, emotions, the control of impulsive behavior, and language. For example, there is less brain volume in the frontal cortex and temporal lobes, and problems within the corpus callosum, the band of nerve fibers which connects the left side and the right side of the brain. People with schizophrenia also tend to have larger lateral and third ventricles. The ventricles are spaces within the brain filled with cerebrospinal fluid. 'Cause I don't use those terms a lot. A contraction is a word that is made up of two or more words that are connected together. One or more letters are removed from the words when they are connected. One or more apostrophes are added in the location letters are removed. For example, an apostrophe goes in place of the o in "not" when "do not" is shortened to "don't".
