Extracting a given number of characters from a cells, counting from left Sometimes you want to extract and use a portion of the contents of a cell, either number or text. Let me give you an example. Say you have entered birth dates in the following format "yyyy-mm-dd", 2004-03-13. It is quite difficult to extract the year of birth here, especially if you have 13 000 of them... This is where the magic of =LEFT() comes in... Go to cell A1 and enter "Date of birth" -- without the "-s. Enter these numbers in the cells below: Go to cell B1 and enter "Year of birth". As you now can see, the cells A1 and B1 both act as column headings -- they describe what kind of data you expect to find below. Go to cell B2 and type =LEFT(A2;4) and hit [Enter]. By the way: instead of typing A2 above, you can of course use your mouse and click inside cell A2 after you've typed =LEFT( Copy down the cells from B2 to the cells B3 and B4. Do this by selecting cell B2 and grab the handle in the lower right corner of the cell and drag it down until you've covered B4. What happened? Your cell B2 should now read "2002", correct? Let's look a bit closer at what happened here... What happens is that you instruct Calc to get the 4 first characters (in this case numbers) in cell A2 from left. Computer software, also called software, is a set of instructions and documentation that tells a computer what to do or how to perform a task. Software includes all different programs on a computer, such as applications and the operating system. Applications are programs that are designed to perform a specific operation, such as a game or a word processor. The operating system (e.g. Mac OS, Microsoft Windows, Android and various Linux distributions) is a type of software that is used as a platform for running the applications, and controls all user interface tools including display and the keyboard. By following these instructions, you'll create a beautiful, customizable space where memories can be made for years to come. **Materials needed:** 1. Teams change their pitchers often because it is hard for a pitcher to throw a full game of nine innings. A pitcher can sometimes throw a no-hitter where no one on the opposite team gets an earned hit. A team can use as many pitchers as it wants to, but it is rare to use more than eight in a game. The ways that a pitcher throws the ball are called "pitches". Many professional pitchers use two or more different pitches. Pitchers change which pitch they throw so that the batter will be tricked and not know what pitch to expect. This makes it more difficult for the batter to hit the ball. Pitchers can make the ball move differently: faster or slower, closer or farther from the batter, higher or lower. There are also many types of pitches, such as the slurve, curve, slider, splitter, sinker, screw, 2-seam cut, 2-seam screw, knuckle, knuckle curve, change-up, circle change-up, palm ball, and others. Title: How to Complete Your Homework without Feeling Overwhelmed or Nauseous Introduction: Homework can often feel like a daunting task that induces stress and anxiety, leading some individuals to experience feelings of nausea. However, with proper time management, organization, and self-care strategies, you can complete your homework efficiently while minimizing negative emotions. This wiki-style guide will provide a comprehensive set of steps and tips to help you overcome these challenges. Section 1: Time Management Strategies Step 1: Create a Study Schedule Create a daily study schedule that includes dedicated blocks of time for completing homework assignments. Allocate more time for challenging subjects and less time for easier ones. Use tools such as calendars, planners, or digital apps to keep track of due dates and deadlines. Key Tip: Break up larger tasks into smaller, manageable chunks to avoid feeling overwhelmed. Guideline: Aim for consistency by setting aside specific times each day for studying rather than cramming all work into one extended session. Step 2: Prioritize Tasks Determine which assignments are most important based on their complexity and urgency. Accomplish high-priority tasks first before moving onto lower-priority items. Apply techniques like Eisenhower's Urgency/Importance Principle or ABCDE Method to prioritize effectively. Key Tip: If unsure about an assignment's priority level, consult with teachers or professors to clarify expectations. Guideline: Allow extra time for complex projects or exam preparation to prevent last-minute rushes. Section 2: Organizational Techniques Step 3: Gather Materials Collect necessary materials (textbooks, notebooks, writing utensils) before starting homework. Having everything at hand reduces distractions caused by searching for missing resources during study sessions. Key Tip: Keep a designated workspace free from clutter to optimize focus and productivity. Guideline: Store frequently used supplies within easy reach to streamline the process. Step 4: Review Lesson Notes Refer back to class notes, lectures, and discussions to refresh memory and understanding of concepts covered in class. Identifying areas requiring additional review enables targeted learning efforts. Key Tip: Highlight or underline essential information in textbooks and notes to facilitate quick reference later. Guideline: Take breaks between topics to allow absorption and retention of new knowledge. Section 3: Self-Care Practices Step 5: Practice Mindfulness Engage in mindful breathing exercises or meditation before beginning homework to reduce stress levels and improve concentration. Regularly practice mindfulness throughout the study session to maintain mental wellbeing. Key Tip: Utilize guided meditations or relaxation apps to assist in establishing a consistent mindfulness routine. Guideline: Set aside time after completing homework to unwind and disconnect from academic responsibilities. Step 6: Stay Hydrated & Fueled Drink plenty of water and consume nutritious snacks during study breaks to ensure optimal brain function and energy levels. Avoid consuming excessive caffeine or sugary drinks, which may cause jitters or crashes. Key Tip: Prepare healthy snack options ahead of time to encourage smart food choices when hunger strikes. Guideline: Establish regular meal schedules to promote overall health and stability throughout the day. Conclusion: By implementing effective time management strategies, organizational techniques, and self-care practices, learners can minimize feelings of overwhelm and nausea associated with completing homework. Consistently applying these principles fosters a positive relationship with academics, ultimately resulting in greater success and personal satisfaction. A region is a part of the country with a government and a president. Title: How to Automatically Lock Android Apps Android devices offer a variety of features to enhance security and privacy. One such feature is the ability to automatically lock certain apps after a specified period of inactivity. This can be useful if you want to prevent others from accessing your sensitive data or if you simply want an extra layer of protection for your device. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to do this: **Step 1:** Unlock Your Device and Open the Settings App This initial step seems straightforward, but it's important to note that you need to unlock your device using your preferred method (fingerprint, pattern, password, etc.). This ensures that only authorized users can modify the settings. Once unlocked, locate and open theSettings app - usually found on the home screen or in the app drawer. **Step 2:** Navigate to Security & Privacy Settings In most versions of Android, these settings are located under the 'Security & Privacy' section. However, depending upon your manufacturer and version of Android, the exact location may vary slightly. Look for categories like 'Personal,' 'Privacy,' or similar terms related to securing your device. **Step 3:** Find and Tap 'App Locks' Option Once you find the security settings, look for an option labeled as 'App locks', 'Lock apps,' or something similarly descriptive. Tapping on this will take you to a list of all installed applications on your device. **Key Tip:** If you cannot find these options, consider installing third-party apps like 'AppLock' or 'Norton App Lock.' These provide similar functionalities. **Step 4:** Select App(s) You Want to Auto-Lock Scroll through the list of available apps and tap those you wish to auto-lock. A checkmark will appear next to them once selected. It's recommended to choose apps containing personal information or financial details. **Step 5:** Set Up Timeout Duration After selecting the apps, there should be an option to set up a timeout duration. Choose how long the app should remain unlocked before automatically relocking itself due to inactivity. Typical choices range from 30 seconds to 30 minutes. **Guideline:** Choosing shorter durations increases security but might become annoying if you frequently switch between apps. Longer durations reduce inconvenience but compromise safety. Balance both aspects based on your needs. **Step 6:** Confirm Setup by Entering PIN/Pattern/Password To confirm the setup process, you'll likely be asked to reconfirm your current device lock method (PIN, pattern, fingerprint, etc.) This adds another level of authentication, ensuring no one else can alter your chosen settings. **Step 7:** Test Your Configuration Finally, test out your new configuration. Launch one of the locked apps, leave it idle for the defined time interval, then return to see whether it has indeed relocked itself. By following these steps, you've successfully added an additional layer of security to your Android device by automating the locking mechanism for specific apps. Remember to adjust timings according to your comfort level and regularly review which apps are locked as you install or remove software from your phone. [points to the picture to show Mag] = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV1/MT/sopmt1.cha = = = Creating a logo is an important part of establishing your small business or website's brand identity. A well-designed logo can help you build trust with your audience, set yourself apart from competitors, and make a strong first impression. Here is a detailed guide on how to create a professional-looking logo: **Step 1: Define your brand identity** Before you start designing, it's essential to have a clear understanding of your brand's values, mission, target audience, and overall aesthetic. This will help inform the design choices you make and ensure that your logo effectively communicates what your business or website is all about. Consider creating a mood board or gathering inspiration from other sources such as Pinterest or design blogs. **Step 2: Choose the type of logo** There are several types of logos to choose from, including wordmarks (text only), symbol or icon-based logos, combination marks (a mix of text and symbols), and letterforms (stylized letters). The type of logo you choose should align with your brand identity and be appropriate for your industry. For example, a law firm may opt for a more traditional wordmark, while a tech startup might go for a modern, minimalist symbol. **Step 3: Brainstorm ideas** Once you have a good sense of your brand and the type of logo you want, start brainstorming ideas. Think about visual metaphors, shapes, colors, and typography that could represent your business or website. Sketch out rough drafts of your ideas to get a feel for how they look on paper. Don't worry about making them perfect at this stage - just focus on generating a variety of options. **Step 4: Refine your designs** Now it's time to refine your sketches into digital versions using graphic design software like Adobe Illustrator or free alternatives like Canva or Gravit Designer. Experiment with different fonts, colors, and layouts until you find a composition that works well. Remember to keep it simple and legible, especially if you plan on using your logo in smaller sizes. **Step 5: Get feedback** Show your logo designs to friends, family members, colleagues, or even potential customers to gather their thoughts and opinions. Ask specific questions about whether the logo accurately represents your brand, if it stands out, and if there are any elements that could be improved. Use this feedback to further refine your designs. **Step 6: Finalize your logo** Make any final tweaks based on the feedback you received, then export your logo in various file formats (such as .png, .jpg, and .eps) to ensure versatility across different mediums. It's also a good idea to create both horizontal and vertical versions of your logo, as well as black-and-white variations for situations where color isn't an option. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Avoid overly complex designs, which can become illegible when scaled down. * Make sure your logo looks good in both large and small sizes. * Opt for timeless design elements rather than trendy styles that may date quickly. * Test your logo on light and dark backgrounds to ensure readability. * Keep consistent spacing around your logo to maintain its integrity. * When choosing colors, consider the psychology behind them and how they relate to your brand. * Always use vector graphics software (like Adobe Illustrator) to create your logo, so it remains sharp regardless of size. By following these steps and guidelines, you'll be able to create a professional-looking logo that effectively communicates your brand's message and sets you up for success. Title: How to Properly Dispose of Unused or Expired Liquid Medication Introduction: Proper disposal of unused or expired liquid medication is crucial to prevent accidental ingestion, environmental pollution, and abuse. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on safely disposing of such medications, following FDA (Food and Drug Administration) recommendations and EPA (Environmental Protection Agency) guidelines. **Important Note**: Always check local regulations as some areas may have specific requirements for medication disposal. Step 1: Mix the Medication With Undesirable Substances To make the medication less appealing and harder to extract, mix it with undesirable substances like cat litter, coffee grounds, or dirt. Make sure the mixture is large enough that someone won't be able to remove and consume the medicine accidentally. Key Tip: Use a sealable plastic bag to contain this mixture before throwing it away. This extra layer of protection prevents leakage and potential exposure. Why It Helps: By making the medication unappealing and difficult to isolate, you minimize the risk of accidental consumption by humans or pets. The added layers also ensure safe transportation and disposal without spillage. Step 2: Seal the Mixture in a Container Place the mixed medication into an impermeable container, such as a sealed plastic bag or empty pill bottle. This ensures nothing leaks out during transport or storage prior to disposal. Guideline: If using a used pill bottle, remember to remove any personal information from the label first to protect your privacy and identity. Why It Helps: Securely containing the mixture reduces the chance of contamination in landfills and water sources due to leakage. Step 3: Throw Away the Contained Medication Discard the sealed bag or container in your household trash. Be sure to keep it out of reach of children, pets, and others who might go through your garbage. Key Tips: - Cut off any sharp edges of bottles or containers to avoid injury. - Mark the discarded item as "expired" or "unused medication" if possible, so waste handlers are aware of its contents. Why It Helps: Putting the contained medication in the regular trash allows it to decompose naturally over time while minimizing risks associated with improper handling or accidental ingestion. Alternative Step 4: Utilize Medicine Take-Back Programs (Optional) Check if there are any medicine take-back programs available in your community. These initiatives allow individuals to drop off their unwanted medicines at designated locations for proper disposal. Benefit: Medicine take-back programs guarantee secure destruction of unused medications and reduce the likelihood of environmental harm caused by pharmaceutical pollution. Conclusion: By following these steps, you can help ensure the safe and responsible disposal of unused or expired liquid medication. Remember always to consult local regulations regarding medication disposal methods in your area. And then their mom reached for the purse. Here she goes up up up. Come over while I finish your hair. [as a marker falls to the floor] Title: How to Construct an Equilateral Triangle Inscribed Inside a Circle Introduction: In geometry, an equilateral triangle is a regular polygon with all three sides and three internal angles equal. When such a triangle is inscribed inside a circle, its vertices (corners) touch the circumference of the circle, and the radius of the circle becomes the altitude or height of the triangle. This article will guide you through constructing an equilateral triangle inscribed within a given circle using only a compass and straightedge (also known as a ruler without markings). Materials Needed: - Compass - Straightedge Step 1: Draw a Circle Using your compass, draw a circle on your paper. Any size of circle can be used; however, larger circles may make construction easier due to their increased surface area. For this exercise, let's assume we have already drawn our circle. Key Tip: Make sure that your circle has a consistent width throughout, meaning the distance between the needle point and pencil tip should remain constant while drawing the circle. Step 2: Choose any Point on the Circumference Select any single point along the circumference of the circle. We'll call this Point A. It doesn't matter which point you choose since we are creating a regular polygon - one where all sides and angles are identical. Step 3: Set Compass Distance to Half the Circle's Diameter Adjust the opening width of your compass so that it spans exactly half the diameter of the circle. To do this, measure the diameter of the circle by placing the needle point at the center of the circle and extending the pencil lead to touch the edge of the circle twice, once on either side of the center. Then, adjust your compass width accordingly. Guideline: The compass setting needs to be precise here because it determines the length of the triangle's sides. If unsure about precision, consider measuring the diameter multiple times for accuracy. Step 4: Mark Two Points Along the Circumference Place the needle point back at the center of the circle. Swing the compass around to create two arcs intersecting the original circle at points B and C. These new points should both be equally distanced from Point A along the circumference, ensuring congruency among the planned triangle's sides. Key Tip: Keep the compass setting unchanged during these steps to maintain consistency when locating points B and C. Step 5: Connect Points A, B, and C Take your straightedge and connect Points A, B, and C sequentially. You now have constructed an equilateral triangle inscribed within the initial circle! Confirmation Checklist: To ensure your construction was successful, review the following criteria: 1. All three corners of the triangle (A, B, and C) lie along the circumference of the same circle. 2. Each side of the triangle has the same length, measured as half the diameter of the circle. 3. Angles ABC, CBD, and DCA are all equal, each measuring approximately 60 degrees. Congratulations! By accurately completing these steps, you have successfully created an equilateral triangle inscribed within a circle solely with the aid of a compass and straightedge. He went up and down the street before this house with the irresolution of a gallant who dares not venture into the presence of the mistress whom he loves for the first time, easy of access though she may be; but after a sufficiently long interval of hesitation, he at last crossed the threshold and inquired of an old woman, who was sweeping out a large room on the ground floor, whether Master Porbus was within. Receiving a reply in the affirmative, the young man went slowly up the staircase, like a gentleman but newly come to court, and doubtful as to his reception by the king. He came to a stand once more on the landing at the head of the stairs, and again he hesitated before raising his hand to the grotesque knocker on the door of the studio, where doubtless the painter was at work--Master Porbus, sometime painter in ordinary to Henri IV till Mary de' Medici took Rubens into favor. The young man felt deeply stirred by an emotion that must thrill the hearts of all great artists when, in the pride of their youth and their first love of art, they come into the presence of a master or stand before a masterpiece. Title: How to Have a Good Story Plot When Writing a Story ====================================================== A good story plot is essential for keeping your readers engaged and invested in your writing. A well-thought-out plot provides structure, direction, and purpose to your narrative while also offering opportunities for character development, thematic exploration, and emotional resonance. This wikiHow will guide you through creating an engaging and effective story plot using clear, actionable steps. ### Step 1: Define Your Genre and Target Audience **Explanation:** Determining your genre and target audience sets the foundation for crafting a fitting plot that caters to specific reader expectations. By understanding these elements, you can tailor your plot accordingly, ensuring maximum engagement and satisfaction. * **Key Tips & Guidelines:** + Identify primary and secondary genres (e.g., fantasy with romance). + Research popular tropes and conventions within chosen genres. + Consider age range, interests, and preferences of your intended readership. ### Step 2: Develop Interesting Characters **Explanation:** Strong characters drive stories forward by providing motivation, conflict, and growth throughout the narrative. Creating compelling protagonists, antagonists, and supporting casts ensures that your plot remains dynamic and captivating. * **Key Tips & Guidelines:** + Create multi-dimensional characters with strengths, weaknesses, goals, and conflicts. + Establish relationships between characters early on to build tension and investment. + Use diverse backgrounds and perspectives to enrich your story world. ### Step 3: Craft a Captivating Hook What do you want me to make? Land ProblemMath brain teasers require computations to solve. A, B, and C each had land: am^2 (m^2=square meters), bm^2, and cm^2 (this is c square meter, not square centimeter!) big. A sold 1/4 of his land to B, then B sold 1/3 of the land he had (after he bought land from A) to C. If A, B, and C now have the same amount of land, what does (b+c)/a equal to? HintI advise that you use a letter (ex: k) to compare the three measures. (and just in case, 1/3 is read as one-third, and so on.) (multiplication symbols are not written down) a=4/3k, b=7/6k, c=1/2k (b+c)/a = (7k/6 + 1k/2)/(4k/3) = (10k/6)(3/4k) = 5/4 See another brain teaser just like this one... Title: How to Perform a Cutback on a Skateboard A cutback is a fundamental skateboarding maneuver that involves turning back towards the breaking wave or curve in the road while riding. This move allows surfers and skaters to maintain their speed and control as they change direction. Here's an easy-to-follow guide on performing a perfect cutback on your skateboard. **Things You'll Need:** * A smooth, paved surface (preferably with a slight incline) * Proper safety gear including helmet, knee pads, elbow pads, and wrist guards * A well-maintained skateboard suitable for carving **Step 1: Familiarize Yourself With Your Board** Before attempting any tricks, ensure you are comfortable with your board and can balance effectively. Practice simple exercises like pushing off, coasting, and balancing without putting your foot down. **Step 2: Find an Appropriate Spot** Find a location free from traffic where there's enough space to practice turns safely. An empty parking lot or park pathway would be ideal. Ideally, look for a spot with a gentle slope which will help generate more speed required for this trick. **Step 3: Gear Up** Always wear appropriate protective gear when practicing new skills. It reduces injury risk significantly if things don't go according to plan. **Step 4: Adopt the Right Stance** Stand on your skateboard with your feet shoulder-width apart, positioned slightly over the bolts securing the trucks to the deck - referred to as the 'stance zone'. Position your front foot at around a 45-degree angle and your back foot perpendicular to the board centerline. **Step 5: Get Moving** Push away from the stationary position using your rear leg until you reach your preferred cruising speed. Keep both knees bent slightly for better stability and shock absorption upon landing jumps or impacts. **Step 6: Initiate The Carve** Lean into the turn by shifting your weight onto your heels or toes depending on whether you want to turn left or right respectively. Start slowly; once confident, increase the degree of tilt gradually. **Key Tip**: Focus your gaze ahead in the direction you wish to travel. Our bodies tend to naturally follow our line of sight. **Step 7: Beginning the Cutback** As you approach the end of your first carved arc, prepare to initiate the cutback. To do so, transfer most of your bodyweight onto your back foot, causing the nose of the board to rise slightly. At the same time, rotate your upper torso counter to the current trajectory but keep your lower body facing forward momentarily. **Step 8: Completing the Turn** Now quickly twist your hips in sync with your shoulders, pointing them in the new intended direction. As you perform this motion, simultaneously put pressure on your front foot to level out the board again. Remember to bend your knees deeply during these transitions for optimal balance and control. **Step 9: Regaining Speed After the Cutback** After completing the turn, you might notice reduced momentum due to changing directions abruptly. Pump your legs rhythmically along the contours of the terrain to regain lost speed just like surfing waves. Use small bumps and dips in the ground to propel yourself forward. **Guideline**: Regularly practice pumping technique even outside of doing cutbacks to improve overall flow and efficiency. With consistent practice following these steps, mastering the art of cutting back won't take long. Enjoy exploring different lines and paths! English spelling reform has been proposed by many people since Webster, such as George Bernard Shaw, who proposed a new phonetic alphabet for English. In some cases Webster's changes have been widely adopted in Britain: the spelling "programme" came from the French; US "program" is clearly simpler, and more consistent with word endings in English. In our modern world, English orthography is still a problem. In some countries (notably, France) a national committee can give advice and direction as to spelling. Dressing for petites can be both challenging and fun. The right clothing choices can enhance your figure, making you look taller and more proportionate. This article will provide you with three effective ways to dress if you are petite, ensuring that you look stylish, confident, and chic at all times. **Top 3 Ways to Dress If You Are Petite:** 1. Choose clothes that fit perfectly: Properly fitted garments create an illusion of height by accentuating your body's natural shape without overwhelming or swamping your frame. * **Step 1**: Understand your measurements. Determine your bust, waist, hip, and inseam sizes accurately. Use these numbers when shopping online or as reference points while trying on clothes in-store. * **Key tip**: Avoid buying oversized clothes, which can make you appear shorter than you actually are. Instead, opt for pieces that skim your curves rather than clinging too tightly or hanging loosely. 2. Master vertical lines: Vertical elements draw attention upwards, creating the appearance of length and height. Implement this technique using various design features like stripes, seams, and patterns. * **Step 1**: Select clothing items featuring vertical details such as pinstripes, narrow panels, or piped edges down the sides. These subtle additions elongate your silhouette. * **Step 2**: Opt for monochromatic outfits consisting of similar shades from head to toe. By minimizing contrast between different parts of your outfit, you create one continuous line, enhancing your overall stature. * **Key guideline**: Be cautious about horizontal lines, especially around the midsection, as they tend to cut across the body horizontally, potentially shortening the appearance of your torso. 3. Strategically place accessories: Accessories have the power to elevate any ensemble, but their placement is crucial when dressing for a petite frame. Carefully chosen jewelry, bags, shoes, and hats can further emphasize your newfound elegance and sophistication. * **Step 1**: When wearing necklaces, choose delicate styles that fall near your collarbone or slightly below. Long chains may overwhelm your small frame. * **Step 2**: Keep handbags compact and avoid carrying large totes or backpacks, which might dwarf your size. A medium-sized shoulder bag or crossbody purse works well for everyday wear. * **Step 3**: Shoe selection plays a significant role in adding perceived height. Go for heels or wedges with a slim profile, avoiding chunky platforms. Also, consider nude colors that blend seamlessly with your skin tone, giving the impression of longer legs. * **Optional Step**: Add a hat! Hats can help balance proportions by drawing focus upward towards your face. Just ensure it complements your outfit and doesn't obscure your features. By following these top three ways to dress if you are petite, you'll learn how to select flattering clothing options, master the art of incorporating vertical lines, and strategically utilize accessories to maximize your visual impact. With practice, patience, and experimentation, you'll discover what looks best on you - ultimately boosting confidence and self-expression through fashion. Guess I can ask you some questions now. Imagine you have a bunch of test scores from a class of students. The scores are all positive numbers and follow a lognormal distribution, which means they are skewed to the right with a long tail. This makes sense because there are probably fewer high scores than low scores. Now, let's say we want to create a new set of scores that is skewed to the left instead of the right. We can do this by taking the original scores and subtracting them from a really big number, like 1000. This will flip the distribution upside down, creating a left-skewed distribution where most of the scores are close to 1000 and there are fewer lower scores. But wait! These new scores are actually negative numbers now, since we subtracted them from a large positive number. That's okay though, because sometimes in math and statistics we deal with negative numbers. In fact, this type of distribution is called a reflected lognormal distribution, since it's just the original lognormal distribution flipped over a reflecting line. To make things even easier to understand, imagine you have a stack of blocks that represent the original test scores. You can flip the stack upside down to get a new arrangement of blocks that looks different but still follows some rules. This is similar to how the reflected lognormal distribution works! As for fitting this new distribution to a curve, one option is to use something called a beta distribution. This is a type of statistical model that can fit many different shapes of distributions. Since our reflected lognormal distribution has values between 0 and 1, the beta distribution is a good choice because it also deals with values in that range. However, there may be other ways to fit the data depending on its specific shape and characteristics. there are all kinds of other toys over here. You don't want to go on a sail boat? Rationale: In order to read and spell words children must have "This is the ability to identify phonemes or vocal gestures from which words are constructed, when they are found in their natural context-spoken words." This lesson will help children recognize the /i/ sound. They will learn this through practice and writing activities. Materials: Primary paper and pencil, Tin Man Can Fix It by Sheila Cushman (phonics readers), pictures cut from magazines (fish, lip, dish, mitt), chalk, chalkboard and a worksheet of pseudo words (lig,clib,gib,blid,crig,id,kip,ip,hig). 1. Introduce the lesson by explaining to the children that they are going to learn a new mouth move. "Today class we are going to talk about one new mouth move /i/." "At first it may seem tricky and kind of hard, but we will get lots of practice at saying it, writing it and finding it in words." 2. I will explain to them and show them the phoneme /i/. "Class, has anyone ever seen a bug and gone /i/?" I will model for them this sound. "Can all of you go /i/?" 3. "Lets try a tongue twister with /i/ mouth move." Icabod's igloo is icky. "Okay class, you try to say it." "Good, lets do it one more time, but faster." "Lets stretch out the /i/ in all the words." Model this for children. "IIIcabod iiiigloo iiiis iicky." "Okay, children now you do the same thing." 4. "I am going to show you some pictures and I want you to tell me which one has /i/ sound in it." The teacher holds up a picture of a bat and a fish. "Good, lets try a few more." The teacher will hold up several pictures. 5. "Now students it is time to practice writing the lowercase i." "Look up at the board and watch me." "You start your pencil on the sidewalk and make a line up to the road and place a dot above the road." "Okay class, I want you to get out your paper and pencil and practice." 6. "Okay, children we are going to practice spelling words with the phoneme /i/." [Teacher will write large squares on the board.] "Okay, we are going to use these boxes to spell words using our mouth moves." "Each box will hold one mouth move." "For example, if we spell sit, you hear three mouth moves so, we will use three boxes, one for each letter." The teacher would ask the students to spell it, in, fit, fix, bit, spin, and clip using the letterboxes. 7. I will read Tin Man Can Fit It. I will read it a second time, but this time have them raise their hands when they hear /i/. Teacher will write the words on the board. 8. For assessment: Give them pseudo words to pronounce out loud to check for awareness of /i/. One particularly destructive mob is the Creeper - known for its explosive nature that causes significant damage to structures and players alike. This tutorial will guide you through creating an easy yet effective obsidian trap to safely capture these pesky creatures, providing you with a source of gunpowder without any risk. **Materials Required:** 1. Cobblestones or lava buckets (for generating obsidian) 2. Water buckets 3. Flint and steel or fire charge (to create obsidian) 4. Pickaxe (to mine the obsidian) 5. Building blocks (such as dirt or cobblestone) 6. A pressure plate 7. Optional: Torches, buttons, redstone dust, and levers for customization **Step 1: Gather Materials** The first step in constructing your obsidian trap is to collect all necessary materials. You'll need at least eight cobblestone blocks and either water buckets or access to a nearby water source for creating obsidian. Additionally, gather flint and steel or a fire charge to harden the cobblestone into obsidian. Lastly, ensure you have enough building blocks, such as dirt or cobblestone, to build up the walls surrounding your trap. **Step 2: Create Your Obsidian Framework** Find a suitable location for your creeper farm, preferably close to where creepers spawn naturally. Dig out a square area large enough to accommodate your final design - typically four by four blocks. Next, fill two diagonal corners of this hole with cobblestone while leaving the other two corners empty. Pour water from one corner diagonally across the hole so that it flows along the cobblestone edges and meets in the center, forming a small pool. Use flint and steel or a fire charge to ignite the water stream, turning it into steam and solidifying the cobblestone into obsidian. Drain excess water if needed. ![Obsidian Framework]( Okay, Martin you wanna play with the book or the truck? Rogers entered seminary after college but wanted to work with television. Title: How to Perform a Saucer Pass in Ice Hockey Ice hockey is a fast-paced sport that requires skill, precision, and teamwork. One essential skill every ice hockey player should master is the saucer pass. This type of pass allows the puck to glide smoothly over an opponent's stick or body, enabling quick transitions and breaks. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to do a saucer pass like a pro. ### Step 1: Understand the Concept A saucer pass involves lifting the blade of your stick so that the puck travels high in the air, descending onto your target's stick like a frisbee. The name comes from its resemblance to a flying saucer. It's particularly useful when there are defenders between you and your recipient because it goes above their sticks. ### Step 2: Grip Your Stick Correctly Hold your stick firmly but comfortably. For most players, this means using a neutral grip - neither too tight nor too loose. Place your bottom hand (for righties, this would be your left hand) about two feet up from the end of the shaft. Your top hand (right hand for righties) should be placed around your navel level. Remember, you'll need control over your stick to execute a successful saucer pass. ### Step 3: Positioning Your Stick Angle your stick slightly open towards the intended direction of the pass. Keep the curve facing upwards. You want the puck to make contact with the middle part of the blade where it naturally curves upwards. ### Step 4: Loading the Puck Before making the actual pass, load the puck by pushing it into the heel of your blade. This action creates potential energy which will help lift the puck off the ice during the pass. ### Step 5: Snap Your Wrist As you push the puck forward, quickly snap your wrist downward while simultaneously raising the toe of your stick. Think of flicking the puck as if you were turning a door knob. This motion gives the puck the necessary upward trajectory. ### Step 6: Follow Through After releasing the puck, continue your swing through completely. Following through ensures maximum power and accuracy. It also reduces strain on your wrists and arms. ### Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. **Eye Contact**: Maintain eye contact with your receiver throughout the process. This helps ensure accurate passing. 2. **Practice**: Like any other skill, practice makes perfect. Spend time at practice sessions honing your saucer pass technique. Start slow, then gradually increase speed as you gain confidence. 3. **Power Source**: Don't rely solely on arm strength; instead, use your legs and core muscles for added power. Bend your knees and rotate your torso as you release the puck. 4. **Timing Is Everything**: A well-timed saucer pass can catch opponents off guard, creating scoring opportunities. Practice timing passes to coincide with your teammate's movements. 5. **Variation**: Mix up your passes during games to keep opponents guessing. Using different types of passes - including saucer passes - keeps the game exciting and unpredictable. Remember, patience and persistence are crucial when learning new skills. Stay committed to practicing these steps regularly, and soon enough, executing a smooth saucer pass will become second nature. Title: How to Hold a Leopard Gecko Introduction: Leopard geckos are small lizards that make great pets due to their friendly and docile nature. However, handling them properly is crucial to ensure both your safety and the well-being of your pet. This tutorial will provide you with an in-depth guide on how to hold a leopard gecko correctly and safely. Step 1: Wait until your leopard gecko is comfortable with its environment Before attempting to handle your leopard gecko, allow it time to adjust to its new surroundings. A newly acquired gecko may take several days or even weeks to become accustomed to its tank and owner. Regularly interacting with your gecko by talking softly near its enclosure can help speed up this process. Key Tip: Never try to forcefully grab or pick up a frightened or stressed leopard gecko as it could result in injury to either party. Patience is essential when bonding with these creatures. Step 2: Clean your hands before handling Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water before reaching into the enclosure. Not only does this remove any potential bacteria from your skin that might harm your gecko but also eliminates any human scent which could cause stress. Step 3: Approach slowly and calmly When approaching your leopard gecko, move slowly and speak gently so as not to startle it. Avoid making sudden movements or loud noises, which could scare off your gecko. Step 4: Encourage your leopard gecko to come out of hiding Most leopard geckos have favorite hiding spots within their tanks. Gently encourage yours to emerge by placing food items like mealworms at the entrance of the hideout. Once they feel safe enough, they'll venture out to investigate. Step 5: Use two hands to pick up your leopard gecko Place one hand under the belly of the gecko while supporting its rear legs with the other hand. This method prevents unnecessary strain on its spine and reduces the risk of falling. Step 6: Lift gently and steadily Once you have secured a firm yet gentle grip, lift your leopard gecko straight upwards. Keep him close to your body to make him feel secure and prevent accidental drops. Guideline: Remember never to squeeze too tightly; instead maintain a light but steady grasp. Step 7: Provide support during holding Support your leopard gecko's full weight using both hands. Maintain a horizontal position parallel to the ground. This ensures stability and comfort for your gecko. Step 8: Interact carefully Talk softly to your gecko and avoid any quick or jerky motions. Allow your gecko to explore your hands freely without fear of being dropped or harmed. Step 9: Know when to put your leopard gecko back If your gecko starts showing signs of distress such as hissing, biting, or trying to escape, return him immediately to his habitat. Overhandling can lead to undue stress for your reptile friend. Conclusion: Handling a leopard gecko requires patience, care, and understanding. By following these steps, you're ensuring a positive experience for both you and your pet. Always remember, building trust takes time, so don't rush things. Happy herping! Use the Law of Cosines for SSA The Law of Cosines works well for solving triangles when you have two sides and an angle, but the angle isn't between the two sides. In this case, the Law of Sines isn't an option. Also, to solve a triangle that is SSA (or side-side-angle) using the Law of Cosines, you have to be careful to find the correct triangle - there are two possibilities. Drawing a picture helps explain why the situation may have more than one answer. When you use this setup in an actual application, the correct answer is usually pretty clear. The situation is that you know the measures of two of the sides to be 93 and 85 units. The angle opposite the side measuring 85 has a measure of 61 degrees. You see two ways of drawing the corresponding picture. Find the missing parts of the triangle ABC that has sides a and b measuring 85 and 93, respectively, and angle A measuring 61 degrees. The preceding figure presents the situation. Find the length of side c by using the law of cosines with a on the left-hand side of the equation. Use this form because after you input the known values, it's the only one that will have just one variable to solve for - even though that variable has two powers. Enter the values into the law of cosines. Simplify the equation by performing all the operations and getting the variables alone on the right side. You end up with a quadratic equation. Use either the quadratic formula or a calculator to determine the solutions. 0 = c2 - 90.21c + 1424 c = 69.813 or 20.397 So c measures either about 70 or about 20. Let c measure 70, and find the measures of the other two angles. This time, take a departure from the law of cosines and use, instead, the law of sines. Use angle A and side a, and pair the ratio with angle C and side c to get Now multiply each side by 70, and solve for the sine of C. Solve for the angle with that sine. C = sin-1(0.721) = 46.137 The measure of angle C is about 46 degrees. If angle A is 61 degrees and angle C is 46 degrees, then angle B is 180 degrees minus the sum of A and C: 180 - (61 + 46) = 180 - 107 = 73 degrees. Now let c measure 20, and find the measures of the other two angles. Go back to the law of cosines to do this part. You can compare the two methods - the one in this step and the one in Step 2 - to see which one you like better. Use the law with c on the left-hand side of the equation to solve for the cosine of angle C. Use a calculator to find the measure of angle C. C = cos-1(0.979) = 11.763 Angle C measures about 12 degrees, which means that angle B is 180 - (61 + 12) = 180 - 73 = 107 degrees. The ambiguous case causes a bit of confusion. Why would you want two answers? The following example may help clear up this mystery. You really don't want two answers. You just want the one that answers your question. Slim and Jim are both sitting at the intersection of two roads, which forms a 50-degree angle. They leave the intersection at the same time - Slim in his old, slow, beat-up pickup truck, and Jim in his nifty-swifty Jeep. When Jim was 400 yards down the road, the two of them were 320 yards apart. How far had Slim driven at that point? You definitely need a picture for this problem, so: You can safely assume that Slim couldn't have gone farther than Jim in his old clunker - unless his truck had hidden powers. Figure out how far Slim drove, the distance from I to S (this example refers to the distance as j to be consistent with the triangle labels) by using the law of cosines. The side s is 400 yards, and angle I is 50 degrees. Write the law of cosines, and replace the letters with the values. This equation simplifies to a quadratic equation with the variable j. Solve the quadratic equation. 0 = j2 - 514.4j + 57,600 Use a calculator or the quadratic formula, and you get two solutions: x = 349.676 and x = 164.723. Either answer gives you a distance smaller than the distance that Jim traveled. Refer to the preceding figure, and choose the answer that appears to be correct, based on what you know. Today, we are going to learn about something called the "Second Isomorphism Theorem." Don't worry if it sounds complicated - we're going to break it down into easy-to-understand parts. First, let's talk about groups. A group is just a collection of things that we can combine together according to certain rules. For example, imagine you have a set of blocks, and you can put two blocks together to make a tower. As long as you follow the rule that you can only add one block on top of another at a time, you have a group! Now, let's talk about the Second Isomorphism Theorem. This theorem talks about two special kinds of groups: normal subgroups and quotient groups. Without getting too technical, a normal subgroup is a subset of a larger group that fits inside the group in a special way, and a quotient group is a way of dividing up a group into smaller pieces. The Second Isomorphism Theorem says that if you take a group G, and find a normal subgroup N inside of it, and then take any other subgroup H of G, you can create a new group out of H and N. Specifically, you can form the group (HN)/N, which is read as "H times N modulo N". But what does this really mean? Well, think of it this way: when you divide numbers, you often get a remainder. In this case, (HN)/N is like finding the remainder when you multiply H and N together and then divide by N. It turns out that this remainder has some special properties that allow us to turn it into its own group, with its own set of rules. So why is N being normal so important? Well, without N being normal, H intersect N wouldn't necessarily be a subgroup of H, and so we couldn't form the group (H intersect N). By requiring N to be normal, we ensure that H intersect N will always be a valid subgroup of H, allowing us to form the group (H intersect N). In summary, the Second Isomorphism Theorem tells us that if we have a group G, and we find a normal subgroup N inside of it, and then take any other subgroup H of G, we can create a new group (HN)/N by finding the remainder when we multiply H and N together and then divide by N. The fact that N is normal ensures that this new group has some nice properties that allow us to study it more easily. Pretty cool, huh?! They went back home and got her purse. It is called "remixing" due to mixing being the putting together of all the parts of a song, and "remixing" being the putting together of the parts of the song differently than the original. [not sure if this is 'no' yet] Disabling the double tap period shortcut on an iPhone is a simple process that can help you avoid accidentally inserting periods while typing. This feature was introduced with iOS 12 as a way to make typing faster and more convenient, but some users may find it annoying or inconvenient. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to disable this feature: 1. Open Settings app: The first step is to locate and open the Settings app on your iPhone home screen. This will give you access to all the settings and configurations available for customizing your device. 2. Scroll down and select General: From the main page of the Settings app, scroll down until you see the "General" option. Select it to proceed to the next step. 3. Locate Keyboard settings: Once you are inside the General section, look for the "Keyboard" option under the "Hardware & Sound" category. It should be near the top of the list. 4. Choose "Text Replacement": After selecting the Keyboard menu, you need to choose "Text Replacement." This feature allows users to create their own text shortcuts to quickly input frequently used phrases or sentences. However, it also includes Apple's default keyboard shortcuts like the double tap period shortcut. 5. Find the ".@" entry: Inside the Text Replacement setting, you will see a list of existing text replacements created by yourself or provided by Apple. Look for the one labeled ".@," which represents the double tap period shortcut. If there are no entries listed here, then the double tap period shortcut has already been disabled or never enabled in the first place. 6. Delete the ".@" entry: To remove the double tap period shortcut, simply swipe left on the ".@" entry and press the red delete button that appears. Confirm if prompted. Removing this entry will prevent the keyboard from automatically adding a period when you double tap the space bar. 7. Test the results (optional): Although not strictly necessary, testing the result after disabling the double tap period shortcut might provide peace of mind. Type any sentence using two spaces between words, and ensure that only a single space gets inserted instead of a period followed by another space. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Keep in mind that removing the ".@" entry does not affect other text replacement features. You can still add your own personalized shorthand entries for quicker typing. * Make sure to test the functionality once you have completed these steps to confirm that the change took effect. * If someone else uses your phone occasionally, inform them about this modification so they don't get confused by the missing double tap period shortcut. * Remember that changing settings back requires repeating these steps but tapping "+ Add Payment Method" rather than deleting the ".@" entry. Adding lighting effects through Adobe Photoshop can greatly enhance the mood and appearance of your image. This tutorial will guide you through the process of adding light effects using various tools and techniques available in Photoshop. **Step 1: Open Your Image in Photoshop** Open Adobe Photoshop and select "File" > "Open" from the top menu bar. Navigate to the location where your image is saved, click on it, then press "Open." **Step 2: Create a New Layer** To keep our original image intact, we'll work on a new layer. Click on the square icon labeled "Create a new layer" at the bottom of the Layers panel or choose "Layer" > "New" > "Layer" from the top menu. Name this layer "Lighting Effects." **Step 3: Select the Brush Tool** Select the brush tool by pressing B on your keyboard or finding it under the Tools panel. Make sure black is set as your foreground color. **Step 4: Apply Basic Lighting** Start painting over areas where you want to add light with the brush tool. Don't worry about precision; we'll refine it later. The aim here is to highlight general areas. Use a soft round brush for softer lights and harder brushes for more defined light sources. **Key Tip:** To change brush size, use [ or ] keys. For hardness, hold Shift while resizing. **Step 5: Duplicate the Lighting Layer** Right-click on the 'Lighting Effects' layer in the Layers panel and select 'Duplicate Layer.' Name the duplicated layer 'Soft Light.' **Step 6: Change Blending Mode** Change the blending mode of the 'Soft Light' layer to 'Soft Light' in the Layers panel dropdown menu. This will make the light effect softer and blend better with the rest of the image. **Step 7: Adjust Opacity (Optional)** If necessary, reduce the opacity of the 'Soft Light' layer to control the intensity of the light effect. You can find the opacity slider in the upper right corner of the Layers panel. **Step 8: Add Shadow** Repeat steps 3-7 but this time, paint shadows instead of light. Choose a darker shade of gray or black for your brush color. Remember that shadows usually appear around the edges of objects facing away from the light source. **Step 9: Refining Details** Use the Eraser tool (E) to remove any unwanted parts of the light or shadow layers. Be careful not to erase too much - maintain balance between light and darkness. **Step 10: Color Adjustment (Optional)** If you wish to give your lighting an colored tint, create a new Gradient Map adjustment layer. Go to Layer > New Adjustment Layer > Gradient Map. Play around with different gradients until you get your desired result. **Step 11: Save Your Work** Once satisfied with your results, save your edited image. Go to File > Save As... and choose your preferred file format. It's recommended to keep a layered PSD version for future editing. Remember, practice makes perfect. Feel free to experiment with these steps and discover what works best for your specific project. Happy editing! = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Parent Child/SLI/PDF/dup24.cha = = = And she beat it with a spoon. Here okay on this side we've got shapes to feel. The word "communism" is not a very specific description of left-wing political organizations. Many political parties calling themselves "communist" may actually be more reformist (supportive of reforms and slow change instead of revolution) than some parties calling themselves "socialists". Many communist parties in Latin America have lost many members because these parties do different things than what they promised once they get into power. In Chile, between 1970-1973, under the left-wing Coalition (groups of parties) of Popular Unity, led by Salvador Allende, the Communist Party of Chile was to the right of the Socialist Party of Chile. This means it was more reformist than the socialist party. As with other fields of engineering, aerospace engineers (who design and oversee the repair of vehicles) must study for several years at a university or college and earn a degree that specializes in aerospace engineering. The course curriculum is designed to ensure critical thinking and inculcate problem-solving thinking for designing and testing aircrafts, missiles and space crafts. Aerospace engineer is responsible for devising project proposals to ensure financial and technical feasibility, ensuring effective progress of various phases in hardware development, integration and evaluation, testing and analyzing new applications for implementation in aerospace systems. Vet projects to ensure they meet the quality benchmark. Assess ongoing projects in terms of safety and feasibility to match desired objectives. Apart from the technical aspects, the job also has some managerial elements that are: Preparing risk assessment plans and elaborate strategic solutions and working on insightful and attractive presentations for prospective business contacts, clients and various stakeholders. Aerospace technicians (those who repair and construct vehicles) will complete a shorter technical course with 'on the job' training that equips them with the skills required to construct and repair said vehicles. Aerospace jobs include astronauts, pilots, and other professionals. She's looking at the XXX isn't she. You'll just height play with them like this. " Why did they put me out this way?" Tom and Mia are playing with blocks. They like to build towers and houses and cars. But Tom wants to build a big castle and Mia wants to build a small farm. They start to argue. "Give me that block, Mia!" Tom says. "I need it for my castle!" "No, Tom, this is my block!" Mia says. "I need it for my farm!" They pull the block from each other. The block is hard and heavy. It slips from their hands and falls on the floor. It makes a loud noise. It breaks into two pieces. "Look what you did, Tom!" Mia cries. "You broke the block!" "It's your fault, Mia!" Tom shouts. "You wouldn't share the block!" They are both angry and sad. They don't want to play anymore. They turn away from each other. Mom hears the noise and comes to see what happened. She sees the broken block and the unhappy children. "What's going on here?" Mom asks. "Why are you fighting?" Tom and Mia tell Mom their story. They both blame each other. They both want Mom to take their side. Mom shakes her head. She is not happy. She picks up the broken block and shows it to them. "Look at this block, Tom and Mia," Mom says. "It was a nice block, but now it's broken. It can't be used for anything. It's not available anymore. Do you know what else is not available when you fight?" Tom and Mia look at Mom. They don't know what she means. "Your friendship," Mom says. "Your friendship is like this block. It's precious and valuable. But if you don't share and care for each other, it can break too. And then you won't have anyone to play with or talk to or hug. Is that what you want?" Tom and Mia shake their heads. They don't want to lose their friendship. They feel sorry for breaking the block and hurting each other. They look at each other. They see the tears in their eyes and the red marks on their lips. They feel bad. "I'm sorry, Tom," Mia says. "I didn't mean to break the block or your lip." "I'm sorry, Mia," Tom says. "I didn't mean to break the block or your lip either." They hug each other. They say they are sorry again. They smile. Mom smiles too. She is happy. She hugs them both. "I'm glad you made up, Tom and Mia," Mom says. "You are good friends. And good friends don't break each other. They help each other and have fun together. Now, let's see if we can fix this block. Maybe we can glue it together. Or maybe we can use it for something else. What do you think?" Tom and Mia think. They have an idea. "Maybe we can use it for a bridge," Tom says. "Or a fence," Mia says. "Or a slide," Tom says. "Or a ramp," Mia says. They laugh. They have many ideas. They are excited. They take the block and go back to their blocks. They start to build together. They make a castle and a farm and a bridge and a fence and a slide and a ramp. They share and care for each other. They are happy. Mom watches them. She is proud. She knows they learned a lesson. She knows they won't break their friendship again. She knows they will always be available for each other. She loves them. long long long long long long long long long long long, long time. They gave me that piece of paper XXX. And I don't know if you remember. oh, here we made two shirts for the baby. Cut each piece of construction paper into 4 parts the same size (fold it first, open the page, and cut on the folded lines) 3. Glue each of the game pieces onto the pieces of cut construction paper to make cards. Let them dry. Play the game: 1. Mix up the cards. 2. Put them face down on a table or the floor. Mix them up again. 3. Turn one over. Turn another over. Does the Super3 match its description? If not, turn them face down again and try again. If you get a match, put the pair-2 cards-to the side. Title: How to Perform a Rapid Swing Kick Performing a rapid swing kick is an impressive martial arts move that requires power, speed, and precision. This kick can be used for self-defense or as part of a choreographed fight routine. Here's a step-by-step guide to help you master this technique. **Step 1: Understand the Movement** A rapid swing kick, also known as a roundhouse kick, involves rotating your body to generate force while extending one leg outwards in a circular motion to strike your target. It combines elements of both strength and flexibility. **Step 2: Get into Position** Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent. Your non-kicking foot (the one closer to your opponent) should pivot throughout the movement, so ensure there's enough space between your feet to turn freely. Keep your weight balanced evenly across both legs. **Step 3: Chamber Your Leg** Before initiating the kick, chamber your kicking leg by bending it at the knee and pulling it back towards your chest. Think about loading up energy in this position; it's similar to winding up before throwing a ball. The degree of bend will vary depending on personal preference and distance from the target. **Step 4: Rotate Your Body** As you begin to extend your leg forward, start rotating your hips and shoulders away from the target. This rotation generates significant torque, adding power to your kick. Remember, most of the power comes from your core rather than just the momentum of the moving leg. **Step 5: Snap Your Leg Outward** Once your body has turned sufficiently, snap your leg outward swiftly, continuing its circular trajectory until it reaches maximum extension. Make sure to keep your toes pointed and locked during the entirety of the kick. Ideally, contact should be made using the side or ball of your foot - avoid hitting with your toe as it may cause injury. **Step 6: Withdrawal & Balance Recovery** After striking the target, quickly retract your leg back to the starting position to regain balance and prepare for potential counterattacks. Ignoring proper withdrawal could leave you off-balance, making subsequent movements difficult. **Key Tips:** * Practice regularly: Like any skill, repetition leads to improvement. Regular practice will enhance muscle memory, coordination, and overall execution quality. * Stretch thoroughly: Flexibility plays a crucial role in performing high kicks like these. Dedicate time daily to stretching hamstrings, hip flexors, glutes, calves, and lower back muscles. * Focus on form over speed initially: While speed is essential in a real combat situation, focusing too much on velocity early on might compromise accuracy and control. Prioritize correct form first, then gradually increase speed once comfortable. * Train safely: Consider wearing protective gear such as shin guards when practicing with partners. Also, always warm up properly prior to training sessions to prevent injuries. **Guidelines:** * Always respect fellow practitioners and train within predetermined boundaries. Refrain from reckless behavior which increases risk of accidents. * Never attempt complex techniques without supervision if you are new to martial arts. Seek guidance from experienced instructors who can provide constructive feedback and correction. * Modify intensity levels based on individual capabilities and limitations. Not everyone possesses identical physical attributes, hence adaptation is necessary. Who gave that right to XXX, and I'll proudly stand up next to you. Will it fit or will it fall? Title: Checking a Fever without a Thermometer **Disclaimer:** This article is intended to provide helpful advice on checking for fever without a thermometer. However, this should not replace professional medical advice or treatment. If you suspect that someone has a high fever or is seriously ill, seek immediate medical attention. Introduction ------------ Fevers are common symptoms of various health conditions such as flu, cold, or other infections. While using a thermometer remains the most accurate way to measure body temperature, there might be situations where one isn't available. Here we will discuss alternative methods to check if someone has a fever based on observable signs and symptoms. ### Method 1: Touch Test Step 1: Feel The Forehead The forehead is often used to determine whether someone feels hotter than usual which could indicate a fever. Gently place your hand on their forehead and see if they feel significantly warmer compared to yourself or others around them. Key Tip: Remember, touch alone may not always be reliable since environmental factors like physical activity level, clothing, and surrounding temperatures can also affect perceived warmth. Step 2: Check Other Parts of Body If unsure about the results from touching the forehead, try feeling the back of their neck, chest, or abdomen. These areas tend to retain heat better and thus offer more consistent readings. Guideline: Be aware that these tests only give an estimation; hence, look out for accompanying signs of illness too. ### Method 2: Assess Behavior Changes Feverish individuals usually exhibit changes in behavior due to discomfort caused by elevated temperatures. Observing any unusual behaviors can help identify potential fevers. Step 3: Monitor Activity Level Decreased energy levels, lethargy, or extreme sleepiness could suggest a possible fever. Keep track of sudden shifts in enthusiasm towards daily activities. Step 4: Observe Appetite Loss Loss of appetite is another sign associated with fever. Note down instances when the person refuses food despite being hungry earlier. Step 5: Watch Out For Irritability Children particularly become irritable when suffering from a fever. Increased fussiness, crying spells, or mood swings might point towards a fever. ### Method 3: Look for Physical Symptoms Besides altered behavior patterns, specific physical manifestations accompany fevers. Recognizing these indicators further assists in confirming a suspected fever. Step 6: Notice Skin Flushing Flushed skin or red cheeks result from increased blood flow near the surface of the skin triggered by higher core temperatures during a fever. Step 7: Examine Shivering or Chills Even though fevers raise overall body temp, chattering teeth and shivering occur because the body attempts to generate additional heat through muscle contractions. Simultaneously, some people experience goosebumps while sweating profusely due to overheating. Step 8: Check Dehydration Signs Dehydration frequently accompanies fevers because the body loses moisture faster trying to cool off. Dry mouth, cracked lips, decreased urination, sunken eyes, or rapid heartbeat warrant closer examination for possible fever. Conclusion ---------- While no substitute exists for a precise thermometer reading, combining multiple techniques mentioned above provides reasonable judgment regarding a probable fever. Always consult healthcare professionals whenever concerned about persistent or severe symptoms. Stay informed, stay safe! Have you heard him do a "sh"? Here if you sit down you can hold the baby. This authenticity draws people towards you naturally. * Guidelines: + Explore various hobbies and find what genuinely excites you. A laboratory thermometer is a tool used in laboratories to measure temperature with high accuracy. It can be partially or fully immersed in the substance being measured. A laboratory thermometer is recognizable by its long stem with a silver bulb at the end. The silver color in the bulb normally indicates the presence of mercury. Mercury expands as the temperature increases, thus raising the reading, while decreasing temperatures contract the mercury, lowering the reading. Mercury-in-glass thermometers are less used in the 21st century as preference is shifted to other types of thermometers, such as digital, alcohol-filled and organic-based thermometers. Alternatively you may give students a printed copy with a list of the ten words. The words can be from a current unit of study, a spelling list, or be tied to a timely theme. For the purpose of explaining this game we will use this list of words tied to winter weather: *blizzard *flurry *freezing *icy *precipitation *scarf *sleet *snowman *snowstorm *toboggan - Write one of these words on your Response Paddle and say to students, "Guess which word I wrote on my paddle?" - Students guess the word by writing it on their paddles. - Now give students the first of three clues about the word you wrote. For example, the first clue might be: "The word I wrote is a two-syllable word." Using the list above, this clue narrows the list of possible words to six. - Ask students to stand up if they written a two-syllable word. Those who have written one or three-syllable words must erase the word and write one that fits this clue. These students remain seated. - Provide a second clue. For example: "The word I wrote is a compound word." This clue narrows the possibilities to two words - snowstorm or snowman. - Tell the students who have written a two-syllable compound word on his or her whiteboard to remain standing and the others to take their seats. - Those who are seated who do not have a compound word must erase their word and write one of the two compound words. - Provide a third clue: "The word I wrote is a synonym for the word blizzard." If you do not have a synonym for blizzard on your paddle, sit down." - Tell the seated students who do not have a synonym for blizzard to erase their word and write the correct word. - The standing students now show everyone including the teacher the word on their Paddles. Those who guessed "snowstorm" from the beginning score one point. - Be sure to check to see that everyone now has "snowstorm" written on their paddle. Chose a new word and continue the game with a different set of clues. For example: * The word I wrote has at least two syllables. * The word I wrote has a suffix. * The root word of the word I wrote is a six-letter word. Continue the game with another set of clues. For example: * The word I wrote has two consecutive letters that are the same. * The word I wrote is a two-syllable word. * The word I wrote can be described as a light snowfall. Continue the game with yet another set of clues. For example: * The word I wrote is either one or two-syllables. * The word wrote is a noun. * The word I wrote is an item of clothing. "Guess Which Word" actively engages all students in understanding the structure and meaning of this list of vocabulary words. At the same time, there is an element of luck which makes the game fun for everyone. Be sure to keep score and offer some kind of reward for high-point-getters. You are eating lunch at a restaurant in the U.S. with a friend, and the restaurant brought you a huge plate of food. You are amazed at how much food American restaurants serve, so you say this. The portion sizes are so much bigger in the U.S.! A "portion" of food is the amount that one person is supposed to eat for a meal. If you want to talk about the amount of food that a restaurant, cafeteria, etc. gives you, you use the phrase "portion size". "Portion size" is the amount of food that is given to each person for a meal. If the weather is really hot, you say that it's "so hot". "So ___" is a way to express an adjective very strongly. When you want to compare two things, you say "___er" or "more ___". So, for example, you'd say "It's hotter in New York than in London." Putting these together, if you want to compare two things, and say that the difference between them is very strong, you say "so much ___er": It's so much hotter in New York than in London. When it's clear what you're comparing to, you don't need to say "than ___". So, if you're from London and visiting New York, you could say: It's so much hotter in New York. When you're talking or writing about the United States, the most common and natural way to refer to the country is to say "the U.S.". This is much more common than calling it "U.S.A." or "the United States". Notice that you always have "the" in front of "U.S.". (Print this lesson) That's what your sissy had for breakfast. Title: How to Build a Scarecrow Building a scarecrow can be a fun and creative activity that also serves a practical purpose in your garden or farm. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to build one. Materials Needed: 1. Old clothes (long sleeved shirt, pants, hat) 2. Straw or hay 3. Pillowcase or cloth for head 4. Twine or rope 5. Stakes or poles 6. Leaves or straw for stuffing 7. Markers or paint for facial features 8. Gloves (optional) Step 1: Choose a Location Decide where you want your scarecrow to stand. It should be somewhere visible but not obstructive, ideally within sight of any plants you wish to protect from birds. Step 2: Prepare the Clothes Gather an old set of clothing - typically consisting of a long-sleeved shirt, pair of trousers, and a hat. These will form the body of your scarecrow. Make sure they are clean enough to avoid attracting pests. Step 3: Create the Body Framework Drive two sturdy stakes into the ground at least a foot apart, depending on the size of your scarecrow. Tie twine between them about waist height to create a 'waist'. This is where the stuffed shirt will hang. Step 4: Make the Torso Stuff the shirt with straw or hay until it's firm, then tie off the bottom opening with twine. Hang the filled shirt onto the twine 'waist' you created earlier. Step 5: Construct the Legs Stuff each leg of the pants individually with straw or hay, tying off both ends once full. Attach these to the lower part of the shirt using more twine. Step 6: Fashion the Head Fill a pillowcase or piece of fabric with leaves or additional straw to make a round shape resembling a head. Securely knot the open end so the filling doesn't fall out. Step 7: Add Facial Features Using markers or non-toxic paint, draw eyes, nose, and mouth onto the head to bring your scarecrow to life! Be as simple or intricate as you like. Step 8: Attach the Head Affix the completed head to the top of the shirt neckline using either glue, nails, or screws, ensuring it's secure enough to withstand windy conditions. Step 9: Position Your Scarecrow Place your finished scarecrow over the stakes or poles, adjusting its position as needed until balanced. You may need to add extra support around the base if it seems unsteady. Key Tips and Guidelines: - Use natural materials wherever possible to deter animals from disturbing your creation. - Consider adding gloves for added detail and protection against bird droppings. - Regularly check up on your scarecrow during storms or high winds to ensure it remains intact. - Remember, creativity has no bounds here; feel free to customize your scarecrow according to themes or seasons! By following these steps and guidelines, you'll have built yourself a fantastic scarecrow that will guard your garden with whimsical charm! Uh but there is boy dolls [laughs]. In 1795 Nicholas Jacques Cont discovered a method of mixing powdered graphite with clay and forming the mixture into rods which were then fired in a kiln. By varying the ratio of graphite to clay, the hardness of the graphite rod could also be varied (the more clay, the harder the pencil, and the lighter the color of the mark). This method of making pencils is still used today. Fronts are some of the basic building blocks of weather. Fronts occur where two large air masses collide at the surface. Fronts are caused by winds moving one air mass away from its birthplace. On Earth, fronts are usually associated with rain, snow, or hail. Severe weather, such as thunderstorms and tornados can occur with fronts on Earth. It remains to be seen whether fronts do the same thing on Mars. It was heavy as Papa's XXX are.
