Title: How to Cool Down Your Lips After Playing the Trumpet Introduction: Playing the trumpet is an enjoyable activity that can bring joy to both the performer and their audience. However, one common issue faced by many trumpeters is dealing with swollen or sore lips after playing due to the constant pressure and friction caused by the mouthpiece. This tutorial will guide you through several steps to help cool down your lips effectively and safely, ensuring they remain healthy for future performances. Step 1: Stop Playing Immediately As soon as you notice discomfort or pain while playing, stop immediately. Continuing to play may worsen the condition, potentially leading to more severe problems such as blisters or cracked skin. Taking breaks during practice sessions allows time for your lips to recover from any stress or strain. Key Tip: Set regular short breaks (every 20-30 minutes) throughout your practice session to prevent overexertion. Step 2: Massage Gently Once you've stopped playing, gently massage your lips using circular motions. This increases blood flow, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. Remember to be gentle; vigorous rubbing could further irritate your already sensitive lips. Guideline: Use only your fingertips, applying light pressure. If it hurts, reduce the intensity of your touch. Step 3: Apply Cold Compress Applying a cold compress can soothe inflamed lip tissue and numb areas where there might be pain. You can use an ice pack wrapped in a cloth, a bag of frozen vegetables, or even just hold an ice cube against your lips. Never place ice directly onto your skin as this can cause damage due to extreme cold. Key Tip: Don't leave the cold compress on for too long at once - aim for about 5-10 minutes per application, then give your lips a break before reapplying if necessary. Step 4: Stay Hydrated Hydration plays a crucial role in maintaining lip health. Drinking plenty of water keeps your body hydrated overall, which in turn helps keep your lips moisturized and less prone to dryness and cracking. Avoid drinks that dehydrate like alcohol and coffee. Did You Know? Dehydration doesn't just come from lack of drinking water but also from sweating profusely during exercise or hot weather conditions. Make sure to replenish lost fluids regularly. Step 5: Moisturize Regularly Use a lip balm or chapstick containing natural ingredients to lock moisture into your lips. Look for products without artificial flavors or fragrances, which can actually dry out your lips more. Petroleum jelly is another good option because it forms a barrier against environmental factors that can dry out your lips. Step 6: Exercise Lip Muscles Just like other muscles, exercising your lip muscles when they are well rested promotes strength and flexibility. Simple exercises include pushing your lips forward and holding them taut for five seconds, or puckering up and releasing slowly. Always ensure your lips feel comfortable doing these exercises; never force anything that causes pain. Step 7: Gradual Return to Practice Slowly ease back into practicing once your lips have returned to normal. Start with shorter periods of playtime and gradually increase duration as tolerated. Rapid return to intense practices may lead to relapse of symptoms. Conclusion: By following these steps, you'll allow your lips ample opportunity to heal and strengthen between playing sessions. Maintaining proper lip care ensures continued enjoyment of playing the trumpet without unnecessary discomfort. [takes egg from MOT and puts baby dinosaur in egg] It is your _sex_ that interests me. Volume 18: Voting in the 2004 Elections History of Voting Many of us take our right to vote for granted. Our state and federal governments, however, have been instrumental in the past in denying different groups of people-including women, African-Americans, young people, people who didn't own land and who couldn't pay poll taxes, and people who couldn't read and write-the right to vote. And throughout history these groups of people have organized, struggled, and fought for their right to vote. This activity presents a brief history of voting rights in this country. Begin with a general discussion of what a democracy is and how it is different from other types of governments. Ask learners, "What does it mean to vote?" After helping students read (on this page), use these questions to discuss each section. Insert the name of each group: - How do you think this group felt when they were not allowed to participate in the voting process? - Who do you think denied this group the right to vote? Why would they want to do that? - Who might have helped this group fight for the vote? Who would have fought against it? - Do you think this group could ever have its right to vote banned again? Why or why not? - How might our country be different today if only White male property owners could vote? - Some say that taking the right to vote from criminals is a racist policy. Do you agree? Why or why not? Do you think it is fair that released felons cannot vote? Why or why not? - Do you think foreign-born people who are permanent residents of the U.S. should be allowed to vote? Why or why not? What If the Government Said You Couldn't Vote? In modern America, almost everyone can vote who wants to. But it wasn't always that way! Here are some of the groups that have been blocked from voting over the past two hundred years. 1. Women. For many years only men were allowed to vote. Women were considered too emotional to make wise choices. It took 75 years of protesting before women won the right to vote through the 19th Amendment to the Constitution in 1920. 2. Poor People. When this country was first founded, only White men who owned land were allowed to vote. Lawmakers believed that only property owners had enough at stake in the country to vote responsibly. By the early 1800s, the property requirement was replaced with a poll tax, which required citizens to pay a special fee in order to vote. Poll taxes were made illegal by the 24th amendment to the Constitution in 1964. 3. Young People. For many years, voting was restricted to adults 21 years and older in some states. During the Vietnam War era, many people argued that if you were old enough to fight and die for your country, you were old enough to vote. The 26th Amendment, passed in 1971, granted the right to vote to everyone 18 or older. 4. People Who Could Not Read and Write. Early in America's history, some states only allowed people who could read or write to vote. State lawmakers believed that only people who could read and write could get the information they needed to make smart choices. Nowadays, there are many ways to get information that do not involve reading and writing. The 1965 Voting Rights Act banned literacy tests. 5. African-Americans. The Constitution did not specifically restrict voting to White people. But it stated that only freemen or people who were not slaves could vote. This made it illegal for most African-Americans to vote until after the Civil War. The 15th Amendment, passed in 1870, allowed Black men (not women) to vote. After that, many states passed new laws to restrict Black voting. Literacy tests, poll taxes, and intimidation were methods used to limit Black voting. Southern states imposed a "grandfather" clause, which said that voters whose grandfathers had voted didn't have to take a literacy test. This benefited White men who could not read, because their grandfathers might have been able to vote. This did not help Black men, however, because their grandfathers would have been slaves and would not have been able to vote anyway. The Voting Rights Act of 1965 did away with all these restrictions on who could vote. It also set up a system to make sure that the new law would be followed. Are There Still People in the United States Who Can't Vote? Yes. Convicted felons in most states can't vote. States vary as to whether they restore this right when people get out of jail. In some states, a felon is allowed to vote again once the sentence is served. Mississippi requires a pardon by the governor before a released felon can vote. Also, people who live in the U.S. but are not citizens of this country cannot vote even though they may work and pay taxes here. History of Voting and What if the Government Said You Couldn't Vote reprinted with permission from the Civic Participation and Community Action Sourcebook, Second edition. Edited by Andy Nash. Title: How to Avoid Crying While Listening to Twenty One Pilots Introduction: Twenty One Pilots is a musical duo known for their emotionally charged lyrics and sound. For some listeners, this emotional intensity can trigger tears or strong emotional responses. If you're one of those people who find themselves welling up while listening to TOP, here are some steps you can take to maintain your composure. Step 1: Understand Your Triggers Identify which songs or lyrics tend to evoke an emotional response. Once you know what specifically triggers your emotions, you can better prepare yourself before listening to those tracks. This understanding is crucial because it allows you to anticipate and manage your reactions. Key Tip: Keep a journal of your feelings after listening to different songs. Over time, patterns will emerge that reveal your personal triggers. Step 2: Listen in Public Places Surrounding yourself with other people can help distract you from the emotional content of the music. The hustle and bustle of public spaces like coffee shops, parks, or shopping malls provide natural distractions, making it harder for you to focus solely on the music. Guideline: Choose busy but safe locations where you feel comfortable spending time. Remember, your goal is not to escape your feelings entirely, but to balance them with external stimuli. Step 3: Practice Deep Breathing Exercises Deep breathing techniques can help regulate your heart rate and calm your nerves, reducing the likelihood of crying. Before starting your playlist, spend five minutes doing deep breaths - inhale slowly through your nose, hold for four seconds, then exhale gently through your mouth. Additional Tips: There are many types of breathing exercises available online; experiment until you find one that suits you best. Step 4: Engage in Active Listening Instead of passively letting the music wash over you, engage actively with the song by analyzing its structure, rhythm, melody, and lyrics. This mental exercise can shift your focus away from the emotional impact towards appreciating the artistry. Step 5: Create a Happy Playlist Counterbalance the intense emotion of TOP by creating a separate playlist full of uplifting, cheerful tunes. Whenever you start feeling overwhelmed, switch to this happier list to lift your spirits. Recommendation: Fill this playlist with genres contrasting with TOP, such as pop, disco, or classic rock. Step 6: Set Boundaries Decide ahead of time how long you'll listen to TOP. Setting boundaries prevents prolonged exposure to emotional triggers, thus minimizing the chance of tears. Suggestion: Start with shorter sessions (10-15 minutes) and gradually increase duration as you become more comfortable. Conclusion: Remember, there's nothing wrong with enjoying sad or emotional music. However, if you want to avoid crying while listening to Twenty One Pilots, these steps offer practical ways to manage your reaction. Above all else, respect your feelings and give yourself permission to enjoy music in whatever way feels right for you. I knew I must go back to Rosamund's house. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV2/ER/geoer2.cha = = = Title: How to Create an iPod Playlist for Fitness Walking ====================================================== Are you tired of using the same old playlists for your fitness walking routine? Creating a specific playlist tailored to your pace can significantly enhance your workout experience by providing motivation, maintaining rhythm, and making the walk more enjoyable. This tutorial will guide you through creating an effective iPod playlist for fitness walking. ### Step 1: Determine Your Ideal Beats Per Minute (BPM) The first step is to figure out what tempo suits your walking speed. A general guideline is to aim for 120-140 beats per minute (BPM), which matches a brisk walking pace. You may need to adjust this range depending on your personal stride length and fitness level. There are various online tools available that can help you find the BPM of songs; however, most music streaming platforms also include BPM information within their song details. Key Tip: Selecting songs with varying tempos throughout your playlist can provide mental stimulation and prevent boredom during longer walks. ### Step 2: Choose Motivational Songs Music has been proven to boost mood and increase endurance during exercise. When selecting tracks for your playlist, consider choosing those that evoke positive emotions and inspire movement. Upbeat genres such as pop, rock, hip hop, or dance often work well. However, if slower-paced tunes help you relax and maintain a steady pace, feel free to incorporate them into your list too! Guidelines: - Opt for songs with lyrics promoting determination, resilience, and self-confidence. - Avoid melancholic or slow ballads that might hinder your energy levels. ### Step 3: Arrange Tracks According to Tempo Now that you have chosen motivating songs within your ideal BPM range, arrange them strategically within your playlist. Start with faster-paced tracks at the beginning to encourage a quick start and gradually decrease the tempo towards the middle portion of your walk. Then, slowly build up the intensity again towards the end of your session to finish strong. Key Tips: - Consider arranging your playlist around landmarks or milestones in your route, like starting fast for the initial hill climb and finishing strong for the final sprint home. - Balance high-energy tracks with moderate ones to avoid exhaustion early on. ### Step 4: Test Out Your New Playlist Before committing to your new playlist entirely, test it out on a shorter walk to ensure the pacing feels comfortable and natural. Make any necessary adjustments based on your observations - perhaps some songs are better suited for warm-up or cool-down periods, while others could serve as energizing mid-walk pick-me-ups. Guidelines: - Monitor your heart rate and breathing patterns during the trial run to assess whether the selected songs align with your target intensity zones. - Time your steps according to the beat of the music to optimize synchronization between body movements and musical rhythms. ### Step 5: Regularly Update Your Playlist Last but not least, keep updating your playlist regularly to maintain interest and excitement during your fitness walks. Adding new songs or switching up the order can make all the difference in keeping your mind engaged and motivated throughout each session. Key Tip: Try incorporating seasonal hits or trending tracks to stay current and inspired. By following these five steps, you'll create an engaging, energetic, and customized iPod playlist perfect for enhancing your fitness walking experience. Happy listening! As we know that angle made by circle is 3600, divide the circle into five equal parts by dividing 360 by 5, we get 72. Thus each sector of circle has 72 degrees, by taking the protractor at the center of circle mark a Point 'A' at 72 degrees. Draw a straight line joining the center and point A. Similarly mark 72 degrees from the line drawn. Now we get 3 points, mark them as B, C, and D points. Now join these points to center of circle using scale. Thus circle is divided into five equal parts. So, whenever we want to divide a circle into 'n' equal parts then divide 360 degrees by 'n' to get degree of each sector. Title: How to Change the Active Partition of Your Hard Disk Introduction: Changing the active partition of your hard disk is an essential task when dealing with multi-boot setups or troubleshooting issues related to boot records. This guide will walk you through changing the active partition using two popular tools: Windows Disk Management and Command Prompt (diskpart utility). Before diving into the process, let's understand some basic concepts about partitions and their role in booting up your computer. A hard drive can be divided into multiple logical sections called partitions. An active partition is a special type of primary partition that contains the system files required by the BIOS to load the operating system during startup. Generally, there should only be one active partition per physical hard drive, but sometimes modifying this setting becomes necessary. Disclaimer: Changing the active partition may result in unintended consequences if not done correctly. Always ensure you have proper backups before making any changes to your system's configuration. The author(s) cannot be held responsible for any damage caused by following these steps. Section 1: Using Windows Disk Management You know what yesterday I saw her do? Marvin Gaye (born Marvin Pentz Gay, Jr., April 2, 1939 - April 1, 1984) was an American soul and R&B singer and songwriter. However, some countries have only overseeing laws. A well-known executive institution is The Gambling Commission in the UK. Its main function is controlling commercial gambling in partnership with people in charge of licensing. An example of strict ruling is the Gambling Law in the USA. The law decides gaming rules for local, state, and federal businesses. In 2018, Swiss citizens voted that gambling should also be allowed on the Internet in the future. A convincing 73% voted in the referendum, in which 1.8 million Swiss people took part. The new Gaming Act was put into effect by the Federal Gaming Board on January 1, 2019. Once upon a time, there was a family that lived next to the ocean. Every day they liked to go down to the beach to look at all the wildlife. One day, they saw something special - a net! The family was so excited and they decided to take the net home with them. When they got back home, the family knew that the net needed to be cleaned. Mom said, "We need to be gentle and take care of this net." So the family worked together to clean the net. The next day the family went back to the beach. They brought their net and threw it into the ocean. Mom said, "If we are gentle, we will soon have some fish for dinner." The family watched with anticipation as the net floated out in the ocean. Minutes passed, then all of a sudden, the family could see a school of fish starting to swim into the net. The family cheered and cheered as the net filled up with fish. In the early 20th century, many Olympic athletes began using drugs to improve their athletic abilities. For example, the winner of the marathon at the 1904 Games, Thomas J. Hicks, was given strychnine and brandy by his coach. The only Olympic death linked to doping happened at the Rome Games of 1960. During the cycling road race, Danish cyclist Knud Enemark Jensen fell from his bicycle and later died. A coroner's inquiry found that he was under the influence of amphetamines. By the mid-1960s, sports federations were starting to ban the use of performance-enhancing drugs. The IOC did likewise in 1967. The IOC created the World Anti-Doping Agency in 1999. The IOC-established drug testing regimen (now known as the Olympic Standard) has set the worldwide benchmark that other sporting federations around the world attempt to emulate. The first Olympic athlete to test positive for the use of performance-enhancing drugs was Hans-Gunnar Liljenwall, a Swedish pentathlete at the 1968 Summer Olympics, who lost his bronze medal for alcohol use. Session 4: Speeds, Rates, Steepness, and Lines (45 minutes) In Parts B and C we examined the proportionality of mixtures and scale models. In this activity, we will take a close look at graphs to learn more about the relationships between distance and time, and between steepness and speed. Note 8 Suppose seven cars are all near an intersection. The graph below show the distances between cars and the intersection as time passes. Study this graph carefully and then try to answer the following questions. In what direction is each car moving in relation to the intersection? Compare the cars' speeds. How do their speeds relate to the steepness of the lines? How could you approximate the cars' speeds using the labels on the axes? Close Tip Does the distance vary directly with time for any of the cars? That is, is the relationship between distance and time proportional for any of the cars? How do you know? Do any of the cars stop during their trips? If so, which cars? Choose one of the seven cars, and describe your trip in this car. Use your imagination! Think of your observations as a passenger or driver in this car, and give the highlights of your trip for these 15 seconds. Include where and when you started the trip and what you saw going on around you -- in front of the car, to the sides, and through the rearview mirror. Go Into a Jeet Kune Do Stance Jeet Kune Do is a martial art concept developed by Bruce Lee. It emphasizes adaptability, economy of motion, and efficiency in its techniques. A crucial aspect of Jeet Kune Do is assuming the correct stance, which allows you to move quickly, defend yourself effectively, and strike with power. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to go into a Jeet Kune Do stance: Step 1: Choose Your Base Stance In Jeet Kune Do, there are two primary stances - the On-Guard Stance (also called the neutral or ready position) and the Fighting Stance. The choice depends on your intention and fighting strategy. We will cover both stances below. Step 2: Get into an On-Guard Stance 1. Start from a natural standing position, feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and body weight evenly distributed between both legs. 2. Keep your spine straight but relaxed, shoulders down and chest open. 3. Position your hands at chin level, elbows tucked close to your torso, and palms facing your opponent. This hand placement protects your face while keeping your arms relaxed for quick movement. 4. Maintain a slight forward lean, shifting about 60% of your bodyweight onto the balls of your feet. This enables rapid mobility and explosive movements. 5. Gaze directly ahead, focusing on your opponent without blinking excessively. This mental state promotes readiness and focus. Key Tips: * Distribute your weight evenly between both legs, avoiding heavy reliance on one leg. * Keep your muscles relaxed yet prepared for action. Step 3: Transition into a Fighting Stance The fighting stance varies based on personal preference; however, the following steps provide a solid foundation: 1. From the On-Guard Stance, pivot on the ball of your lead foot (the foot closer to your opponent). Rotate your hips, torso, and shoulders in unison as if drawing a semicircle with your belly button towards your opponent. 2. Simultaneously extend your lead arm (same side as your lead foot), pointing your index finger towards your opponent's eyes while maintaining a loose fist. This extended arm serves as a range finder and distraction technique. 3. Bring your rear hand back, protecting your jawline while staying relaxed. 4. Adjust your lead foot stance to be narrower than your shoulders, angled diagonally away from your opponent. This angle provides better coverage against attacks and facilitates smooth transitions between offensive and defensive maneuvers. 5. Slightly bend your knees more, lowering your center of gravity for increased stability and power generation during strikes. Guidelines: * Always keep moving in small adjustments to maintain balance and control over distance. * Stay light on your feet, enabling swift evasions and counterattacks. * Practice transitioning smoothly between stances to ensure proficiency under pressure. By mastering these steps, you can efficiently adopt a Jeet Kune Do stance, enhancing your agility, defense capabilities, and striking prowess. Remember that consistent practice leads to improved instinctual reactions, empowering you to fluidly navigate combat situations using Bruce Lee's innovative martial arts philosophy. These changes can protect transgender people from discrimination. For example, transgender people often have a hard time traveling because they may look like one sex but have another on their papers. These changes can also be needed for transgender people to be allowed to marry their spouses in places where it is illegal for people of the same sex to marry. These changes can even protect transgender people from a wide variety of violence. Some transgender people are only recognized when their documents reveal them. Being revealed as transgender can put people in danger because of transphobia (fear and/or hatred of transgender people). [Mark understands now which one is the grass] Title: How to Choose a Tattoo Design Introduction: Getting a tattoo is an exciting decision that requires careful consideration. With so many designs available, selecting the right one can be overwhelming. This step-by-step guide will help you make an informed choice about your new body art. Step 1: Determine the Location of Your Tattoo The location of your tattoo affects its visibility and size. Consider where you want to place your tattoo before choosing a design. For instance, smaller intricate designs are better suited for fingers or behind the ear, while larger pieces look great on arms, legs, chests, or backs. Remember, certain areas may cause more pain than others due to nerve endings and proximity to bones. Key Tip: If this is your first tattoo, starting with less sensitive areas like arms or thighs might be more comfortable. Step 2: Reflect on What You Want to Express Tattoos represent personal beliefs, memories, passions, or identities. Think deeply about what message you wish to convey through your tattoo. It could symbolize something meaningful from your past, present, or future dreams. By identifying these elements, you ensure your chosen design holds significance. Guideline: Avoid impulsive decisions based solely on current trends. A truly memorable tattoo should resonate with who you are at a deeper level. Step 3: Research Different Styles There are numerous tattoo styles including traditional, realism, tribal, watercolor, geometric, dotwork, script, etc. Familiarizing yourself with various genres allows you to identify which ones align best with your preferences. Look up artists' portfolios online to view their work and see which aesthetics appeal to you most. Step 4: Consult Professional Artists Once you have narrowed down your preferred style(s), reach out to professional artists specializing in those fields. Share your ideas; they can provide valuable input regarding feasibility, adaptability, and potential enhancements. Many artists create custom designs tailored specifically to clients' visions. Key Tips: - Check artist credentials and reviews thoroughly. - Don't hesitate to ask questions concerning technique, hygiene practices, pricing, and aftercare procedures. Step 5: Evaluate Potential Designs Examine several options before finalizing your decision. Assess whether the design meets all your criteria - significant, appealing, suitable scale, appropriate complexity level, and compatible with selected placement. Keep in mind that simple often translates as timelessly elegant. Step 6: Sleep On It Take time to mull over your top pick. Letting the concept marinate overnight gives you perspective and reduces likelihood of rushed choices leading to regret later. Step 7: Confirm Your Decision After thorough reflection, if you still feel excited and confident about your chosen design, proceed! But remember, there's no rush; take your time until everything feels just right. Conclusion: Selecting a tattoo design involves thoughtful introspection and research. Following these steps guarantees a well-considered, personally relevant piece of artwork etched onto your skin. Cherishing such a permanent mark requires deep appreciation towards both the process and result. Happy tattoo hunting! But I mean very frequently he'll bye bye. [ROM is making car noises while playing with toy cars] view a plan A Social Studies Activity on Bartering and Trade Title: Exchange City Length of activity: 50 minutes Objectives: Students will demonstrate the process of bartering by exchanging with another child an item that is more valuable to them. Material: Items to be exchanged Letter to parents 1. Tell the students you are planning a day called Exchange City. Explain the Exchange City is a day when the boys and girls bring in items that they would like to exchange with each other. (EXPLAIN!! that the exchange is for real. They will not get back the item that they brought if they choose to exchange it with another child.) 2. The students are to bring in an item that is worth between one or two dollars. Explain to the students that they do not have to exchange their item if they feel there is not another item that is worth as much to them as the one they brought. 3. Before you start the lesson ask the students to set out the item that they choose to bring. 4. Make sure that everyone has brought an item that is between the dollar value. It would not be fair if someone brought an item worth five dollars and everyone else brought one dollar items. 5. Now that you have made sure everyone has the proper items - set a date in which Exchange City will take place. (Once again, explain that the exchange is for good) 6. Send a letter home to the parents explaining what activity you have planned. Encourage parents to come to visit on the day. 7. It is now EXCHANGE CITY DAY!! 8. Explain to the class exactly what a barter system is: Barter is the exchange of goods or services without money. (Introduction) 9. Give a couple of common examples of barter. Ex: On Halloween kids get many kinds of candy. Some they like and some they don't. Normally the child will offer a sibling or a parent the piece of candy they don't like for a piece of candy that their sibling or parent would like to give up. The piece of candy you don't like could be your brother's favorite kind. Ex: Have you ever been to Wal-Mart and wanted a quarter for those machines that sit outside the doors? Thisis another form of barter. You and you sibling could put a quarter in the machine and get something totally different than what is pictured on the advertisement. If you want a ring and your brother wants a bouncy ball and what comes out is just the opposite, this is a chance for you to exchange items. This is called bartering. 10. Now that you have explained what barter is, ask the class to place their items on their desks. Let the students walk around and get a sense of what other students have brought. Once they are seated, you (the teacher) begin the exchange by offering one student the item you brought. This should get things rolling. Announce to the class that they only have 30 minutes to do the exchange. Announce when five minutes remain. 11. While they are participating in Exchange City, walk around the room and listen to the different comments made to each other. 12. When time is up discuss the lesson: (closure) How many students traded their item for the item they wanted most? How many didn't trade at all? Why? Was there anyone who traded more than once to get the item of their choice? 13. Have the class define barter in their journals, comment on the activity, and list three examples of barter they have been involved in. 14. Observe activity and write down comments the student make throughout the exchange. (Assessment) 15. Take pictures throughout the activity and develop them for display in the room. Adaptation/Extensions: Send a letter home to the parents saying that if their child participates in 5 barters within two weeks, they will receive 2 points extra credit. To receive the points, a note must be sent to school with your child and signed describing the exchange that was made. Coulson, E. & McCorkle, S. (1982). Swap Day. However, with time, effort, and self-care, it is possible to move forward and heal from this experience. Here's a step-by-step guide to help you forget the girl you truly love: **Step 1: Allow Yourself to Grieve** Before you can begin to forget about her, you need to allow yourself to feel and process your emotions. It's okay to cry, feel sad, angry, or confused. These are all normal reactions to heartbreak. Suppressing these feelings will only make it harder for you to let go and move on. *Key tip*: Don't rush through this stage; take as much time as you need to grieve. **Step 2: Cut Off All Contact** The next step is to cut off all contact with the girl you want to forget. This includes blocking her number, deleting her social media profiles, avoiding places where she might be, and asking mutual friends not to mention her name around you. The goal here is to create some distance between you two so that you can start healing. *Guideline*: Be consistent with this rule - even if she reaches out to you, do your best to maintain no contact. **Step 3: Focus On Self-Care** Now more than ever, taking care of your physical, emotional, and mental wellbeing should be a priority. Eat healthy foods, exercise regularly, get enough sleep, and engage in activities that bring you joy. When we feel good physically, it becomes easier to manage our emotional state. *Key tip*: Try practicing mindfulness meditation or yoga to help reduce anxiety and stress associated with heartache. **Step 4: Seek Support From Others** Lean on your family members and close friends during this challenging time. Talk about your feelings openly and honestly with them. Sometimes just sharing what you're going through can provide immense relief. They may also offer valuable advice based on their own experiences. *Guideline*: Choose people who genuinely care about you and won't judge you for your situation. **Step 5: Rediscover Old Hobbies Or Find New Ones** Immerse yourself in hobbies and interests that you enjoyed before meeting her. Alternatively, try something new that has caught your attention recently. Engaging in enjoyable activities helps distract your mind from dwelling on negative thoughts about her. *Key tip*: Make sure these hobbies aren't related to her or your relationship, as they could trigger unwanted memories. **Step 6: Practice Positive Affirmations** Replacing negative thoughts about yourself and the situation with positive affirmations can significantly improve your mood and overall perspective. Repeat phrases like "I am worthy of love," "I deserve happiness," or "I am strong enough to overcome this" every day until you believe them wholeheartedly. *Guideline*: Say these affirmations aloud while looking at yourself in the mirror for maximum effect. **Step 7: Set Realistic Expectations** Understand that forgetting someone you deeply cared about doesn't happen overnight. There will be days when you still think about her, but over time, those thoughts will become less frequent and intense. Remember, progress isn't always linear; there will be ups and downs throughout this journey. *Key tip*: Celebrate small victories along the way - such as going a full day without thinking about her - to keep yourself motivated. By following these steps and being patient with yourself, you can eventually learn to forget the girl you truly love and open yourself up to new possibilities. Pepin the Short Charles Martel was succeeded as Mayor of the Palace by his son Pepin, often called Pepin the Short. In 752 A.D., the Pope anointed Pepin the Short as king. This anointing by the Pope was important. To his subjects, it meant that Pepin had the backing of God, or that he was God's chosen ruler. In exchange for the blessing of the Pope, Pepin promised to offer his protection to the Church. At this time, the Church in Rome was under attack by a non-Christian Germanic tribe called the Lombards. The Pope had sought assistance from the Byzantine Empire, but they refused to send aid. This left the Pope with no choice but to turn to the Franks. In 754 A.D., Pepin the Short successfully removed the Lombards from Rome and gave their lands to the Pope. This strengthened the ties between the Catholic Church and the Franks, while the ties between the Church and the Byzantines were weakened. He thought you were a cute little boy. Have you ever had something scary like that happen to you? = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/Interviews/07/5266EC.cha = = = Title: How to Change the Language in Windows 7 Windows 7 allows users to change the display language of their operating system to suit their preferences. This feature is particularly useful when you have recently migrated from a different region or if you prefer using your computer in a language other than English. The process involves several steps that require attention to detail. Here's an in-depth guide on changing the language in Windows 7. **Step 1: Check System Compatibility** Before proceeding with the changes, ensure your version of Windows supports multiple languages. Only certain editions - Home Premium, Professional, Enterprise, and Ultimate - offer this functionality. To check which edition you're running: 1. Click the Start button (the orb located at bottom left corner). 2. Right-click Computer and select Properties. 3. Under System section, look for Windows Edition. If it reads anything but those mentioned above, you may need to upgrade or install a separate language pack via MUI (Multilingual User Interface) depending on Microsoft policies. **Step 2: Download Required Language Packs** If your system meets the criteria, download the necessary language pack(s) from Microsoft's official website. Note down the exact name as you'll be needing it later during installation. Key Tips: - Always download language packs directly from trusted sources like Microsoft's website to avoid potential security risks. - Depending upon your internet connection speed and size of the file, the download might take some time. **Step 3: Install Downloaded Language Pack(s)** Once downloaded, locate the .msi file within File Explorer, right-click it, and choose Install. Follow through the prompts until completion. Repeat this process for all required languages. Guidelines: - During installation, keep your system connected to stable power source to prevent interruptions. - It's recommended to close any unnecessary applications before initiating setup. **Step 4: Set New Display Language** After successful installation, restart your PC. Then, log back into your account and navigate to Control Panel by typing 'Control Panel' in search bar next to start menu and selecting Classic View. 1. Inside Control Panel, click Clock, Language, and Region. 2. Choose Regional and Language Options. 3. Navigate to Keyboards and Languages tab. 4. Click on Change keyboards... button. 5. Switch to the Languages tab in newly opened window. 6. Press Add..., scroll down to find your installed language, expand its node, tick the box beside Display language, then hit OK twice. Now, let's move onto applying these settings: 1. Go back to Regional and Language Options inside Control Panel. 2. Still under Keyboards and Languages tab, press the Download and install language pack... button. 3. Wait while Windows retrieves files needed for display language conversion. 4. Once done, go ahead and select your new language from the dropdown list labeled 'Select display language'. 5. Confirm the action by hitting Apply followed by OK. 6. A popup will appear asking whether to restart now or later; opt for Restart Now since changes won't take effect till reboot. Congratulations! After following these steps, you should now see your chosen language displayed across various elements of the Windows interface including menus, dialog boxes, help contents etc., making interaction more comfortable and personalized. [it is difficult to hear because magnets are being poured out onto a hard surface creating much noise] This was done with second grade students. One of the things in our district curriculum is they have to write a survey question. They have to understand how to survey people and how you get information and put it into different graphs. I gave them some examples of different survey questions. We looked at USA Today. I collected some of the [polls] over the week. You know on the front page, they always have those [polls]. How many Americans like to do what sport? So we looked at some of those to see what are some real life applications of doing a survey. Then the students wrote their own survey questions. They had to come up with four or five choices to go along with their survey. Most of the kids did things like what is your favorite television program? What is your favorite ice cream? What is your favorite candy? Who is your favorite NFL football team? Those types of things. Then, we have clipboards in our classroom so that everybody can have their own clipboard. So I taught them how to do the tally method. You know one, two, three, four, and then you do the diagonal for five. They went outside during lunch recess and there are a lot of different kids outside during that time. A lot of different grades. So they were allowed to go around and survey kids on the playground. So they had their clipboard and their pencil and their piece of paper with their choices on it. So they went around and some kids choose to just ask anybody. Some kids just asked only the second graders that they knew. I said that you could ask as many or as few as you want. We have twenty minutes. "I know it's your recess time, but you're going to do this." They didn't care. They were excited. They were going around. Some kids would end up with the clipboard and this line of people waiting to answer the survey, because they were excited. Once they had that data, they went ahead and tabulated it on their paper. If it is "what is your favorite ice cream", for the chocolate, they would count up their little tallies and then put the number. You know, twenty-three. They tabulated the data on their paper. Then we went to the computer lab. Every student had his/her own computer and we go there for forty-five minutes every day. We went to the spreadsheet program. Remember these are second graders. So I showed them very quickly how you make the spreadsheet - the cells bigger - and how to change the font to a bigger size. I really didn't worry about limiting how many cells that we would see on the page. It was just - if you click in this upper corner - then it would highlight the whole spreadsheet. We were using AppleWorks and then, here's how you change the font, the size, and if you put your little tool in between like the A and the B column. It will change into a ruler. You hold your mouse down and you can drag it out make it bigger. That part of the lesson I did as a whole group. You run around as a teacher. You have the demo computer that shows them what they are supposed to do. All right, everybody open AppleWorks. Everybody go to the spreadsheet. Everybody click here. Check your partners, your spreadsheet should be highlighted. You walk them through how to fix and change all of those things and then save it as the name of their survey. Then, because they had it ready to go, then all they had to do was use their piece of paper and type in the words in the first column. Then their totals in the second column. That took about a day. We spell checked it so that that was in good shape. Then the next day, when we went back, they had to make a graph out of that data. Something that I do in my classroom with them already. We talk about - we only go into bar graphs, pie graphs, and line graphs. I have a poster up that we created together that we use that says pie graphs are parts of a whole. So the information would be part of a whole something. Bar graphs compare things. Line graphs show growth over time. On the second day, when we went back to the computer, we talked. Let's think about the information that we have. Which graph would it fit? Are we showing growth over time? Well, okay, no - nobody is going to use a line graph. Then our only two choices are bar graphs and pie graphs. We talked about well - it could be, if you asked one person's class - it could be part of a whole for that class. Or you could say part of all the children at recess. You could use a bar graph. Either one really would fit. Then the students went in and we did it again I was instructing. Teacher demo up. They were going through the steps. All right everybody you have to highlight that information that you have in your graph. Go to Options. Make a Chart. You can decide, pie graph or bar graph. Then we talked about click on the labels. Let's type in the title. If you want the numbers in, you can click show the numbers. We walked through all of those types of things. Then they made their graph. So we put it on the page a little bit over. Not on top of their words and their numbers. Then just printed that out. When they went to print, they had to [print just] page one. So it wouldn't print eons of this document with just blank cells. So they all had their spreadsheet and then their graph to go along with it. I wanted to teach them spreadsheets and the survey part of it was a part of our curriculum. I just tied the two together. How did you assess it? Did you do things to assess their work? I assessed it on whether they actually had the information in the right cells and whether they made the graph. They had to have the title on it. Then when we went back to the classroom, they all shared. We sat in a circle and they had their little piece of paper. So we shared each one of their surveys. So also to see, could they make comparisons about their information, their data. Could some of them say, well more third graders like chocolate than vanilla? The totals for the Rams and the Bears equal the total for the Bulls. I probably used teams that aren't even in the same league. I don't know my teams. That is how I assessed it. Then we hung it up on the bulletin board for a while and then we took them home. Sounds like what you expected them to learn, they learned in the way of the spreadsheet. How do you feel the project went over all? It's exhausting when you work with younger students on the computer because they need a lot of help. They need you to walk them through it step by step. So there is probably more down time than I would really like to see. But if you are the only person in there with them I didn't have any help. Maybe having a couple of parents or some older students might make things go a little smoother and quicker. It was still a good project and actually we ended up doing some other things with spreadsheets later. So that initial lesson was well spent. So your role was much more involved. Right. We are surrounded with snow and ice. Here's a little activity for the kids. Take a bowl of ice cubes and set it in front of them. Tell them you want them to go fishing for the ice cubes. Their challenge? Pick up an ice cube with a piece of string. We all know that sprinkling a little salt on ice will melt the ice. But how does it work? Salt dissolves in water easily. The salt molecules sodium ion and a chloride ion (sodium chloride) disassociate in the water and break apart. They confuse the water molecules so they can't freeze as easily. To start your fishing trip, start with a wet string and a little salt. Lay the string over the ice cube and sprinkle it with the salt. Wait for a few minutes while the reaction takes place. As the salt melts the ice, the string moves down into the ice a little bit. Then fresh water moves up and refreezes over the string. You can then pick up the ice cube with the string. Think about this experiment on a larger scale - how does sodium chloride or magnesium chloride work on melting ice on the roads in winter? Watch the video for the answer. Hide Your Anime Obsession: A Comprehensive Guide =============================================== Introduction ------------ Anime has become increasingly popular worldwide, captivating audiences with its unique storytelling and stunning visuals. However, some fans may feel uncomfortable sharing their enthusiasm for anime with others due to societal norms or personal preferences. This guide will provide you with detailed steps on how to hide your anime obsession without suppressing your passion entirely. By following these steps, you can maintain a balance between enjoying your hobby privately and interacting socially with people who might not share the same interest. ### Step 1: Be Selective About Public Displays of Affection for Anime #### Explanation Refrain from wearing clothing or accessories that overtly showcase your love for anime in public settings where you are likely to encounter people unfamiliar with the medium. While there is nothing inherently wrong with expressing your fandom through apparel, doing so may unintentionally attract attention or assumptions about your interests. #### Key Tips * Opt for subtle designs or generic graphic tees instead of those featuring specific characters or series. * Limit the display of anime-themed items (e.g., figurines, posters) to private spaces like your bedroom or home office. ### Step 2: Monitor Your Conversations Related to Anime #### Explanation Be mindful of the topics you bring up during conversations with coworkers, acquaintances, and casual friends. Although discussing anime can be an enjoyable way to connect with like-minded individuals, it may alienate those who have little knowledge or appreciation for the medium. #### Guidelines * Avoid initiating discussions about anime unless you know the other person shares similar interests. * If someone asks about your hobbies, respond honestly but tactfully by mentioning anime alongside other activities you enjoy. * Listen actively when others speak about their own pastimes and demonstrate genuine curiosity. ### Step 3: Diversify Your Social Media Presence #### Explanation Curate your social media profiles to reflect a well-rounded personality that goes beyond just anime. This does not mean hiding your love for anime altogether; rather, it involves balancing posts related to anime with content from various aspects of your life. #### Key Tips * Share updates about your career, education, family, and personal achievements. * Post pictures of your travels, favorite foods, and local events to give others insight into your diverse range of interests. * Follow accounts unrelated to anime to broaden your online presence and engage in conversations outside the anime community. ### Step 4: Cultivate Alternative Interests #### Explanation Developing new hobbies or pursuing existing ones can help create a more balanced identity, making it easier to conceal your anime obsession if necessary. Moreover, engaging in alternative activities allows you to meet new people and form connections based on shared passions outside of anime. #### Suggestions * Join clubs, organizations, or groups centered around sports, arts, literature, or volunteering. * Attend workshops, classes, or seminars to learn new skills or deepen your understanding of subjects outside of anime. * Participate in community events, festivals, or cultural celebrations that align with your broader interests. ### Step 5: Establish Boundaries With Like-Minded Individuals #### Explanation What are you going to do with it? I don't want egg I just want toast and bacon and peanut butter. Title: How to Ask Your Boyfriend to Prom Introduction: Prom is an exciting event in every high school student's life. It's natural to want your boyfriend by your side during this memorable occasion. However, asking him can be nerve-wracking. This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to ask your boyfriend to prom, ensuring that your question pops as magically as the big night itself! Step 1: Choose the Right Time and Place ------------------------------------- Timing is crucial when planning to ask your boyfriend to prom. Make sure he isn't preoccupied or stressed about something else at the moment. Pick a time when both of you are relaxed and have some privacy. The location should also be special - somewhere meaningful to both of you such as where you had your first date, his favorite spot, or even at home during a cozy movie night. A thoughtful setting enhances the significance of the moment. Key Tip: Avoid public places if your boyfriend tends to feel embarrassed easily. You don't want him to feel uncomfortable or put on the spot in front of others. Step 2: Decide on How You Want to Ask -------------------------------------- There are countless ways to propose the question. Some popular ideas include creating a scrapbook filled with memories, writing a heartfelt letter, baking a cake, or making a video montage of your best moments together. Alternatively, keep it simple yet romantic by just saying those four words, "Will you go to prom with me?" Personalize your approach based on what suits your relationship dynamic best. Guideline: Whatever method you choose, ensure it reflects your personality and relationship. Authenticity matters more than grandeur. Step 3: Practice What You Will Say ---------------------------------- Rehearse your lines beforehand so you won't stumble over your words or forget anything important when the actual moment arrives. Practicing will help build confidence and reduce anxiety. Key Tip: If using props like a sign or banner, practice holding them up straight and reading aloud from them. Step 4: Prepare His Response ---------------------------- While there's no way to guarantee his answer, preparing for either response shows forethought and consideration. Have two scenarios ready depending on whether he says yes or no. For instance, if he agrees, plan a celebration dinner. If he declines (hopefully unlikely), decide ahead how you would handle rejection gracefully. Guideline: Remember, his feelings matter too. Respect his decision regardless of the outcome. Step 5: Pop the Question ------------------------ Now comes the climax! Take deep breaths, stay calm, maintain eye contact, and then utter those magical words. Whether through speech, action, or prop, let your love shine through. Step 6: Handle His Reaction Calmly ---------------------------------- His reaction might surprise you; excitement, shock, silence, laughter - all normal responses. Give him space to process the situation and respond naturally. Step 7: Celebrate Either Way --------------------------- Whether he accepts or declines, celebrate the courage it took for you to ask. Share ice cream, watch a funny movie, or engage in another activity you both enjoy. After all, bravery deserves recognition! Conclusion ---------- Asking your boyfriend to prom doesn't need to be daunting. With careful planning, sincerity, and respect for his feelings, you can create a beautiful memory that strengthens your bond. Good luck! Title: How to Access Archived Photos on Instagram Instagram's archive feature allows you to hide posts from your profile without deleting them. This can be useful if you want to remove a post temporarily or organize your profile. Here is an in-depth guide on how to access archived photos on Instagram. **Step 1: Open the Instagram App** Start by opening the Instagram app on your mobile device. The icon looks like a multicolored camera lens. If you are not already logged in, enter your username and password when prompted. **Step 2: Go to Your Profile** Tap on the person-shaped icon at the bottom right corner of the screen. This will take you to your profile page where all of your posted content is displayed. **Step 3: Tap on the Three Horizontal Lines (Hamburger Icon)** At the top right corner of the screen, there should be three horizontal lines stacked vertically - this is often referred to as the 'hamburger icon'. Clicking it opens up a menu with several options including 'Archive.' **Step 4: Select Archive** From the dropdown menu, select 'Archive.' It has an clock symbol next to it. Once selected, you'll see two tabs: 'Posts Archive' and 'Stories Archive.' **Step 5: Choose Posts Archive** Click on 'Posts Archive' which shows all your hidden posts arranged in chronological order starting from newest. **Step 6: Browse Through Your Archived Posts** You can now scroll down through your archived posts. They appear exactly as they did when they were live; only difference being that these won't be visible to others on your profile. **Key Tips:** - You cannot view someone else's archived posts even if you have access to their account because archiving is private to individual users. - Each photo in the archive still retains its likes and comments so don't worry about losing any engagement data. - To unarchive a photo, open the archived version, tap the three dots in the upper right corner, then choose "Show on Profile." - Only photos that you've originally posted show up here, shared posts from other accounts do not get archived. By following these steps, you're able to access any photos you've chosen to hide from your profile while keeping them safely stored within the app. Remember, the archive function gives you control over what appears on your public profile without permanently removing any cherished memories or important information. Title: Easily Give a Cat a Pill (Method II) Introduction: Giving a cat medication can be a challenging task due to their natural instincts and behaviors. However, with patience, practice, and the right techniques, you can successfully administer pills to your feline friend without causing them undue stress or harm. This tutorial will guide you through Method II of giving a cat a pill, which involves hiding the medicine inside a tasty treat or food item. Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. Choose an appropriate hiding place: Select a soft, palatable treat or food that your cat enjoys and is unlikely to chew thoroughly before swallowing. Some popular options include: * Small pieces of cooked meat or fish * Soft cheese or cream cheese * Wet cat food or pate * Commercial pill pockets or gel capsules specifically designed for cats 2. Prepare the pill: Before starting, ensure that you have washed your hands thoroughly to avoid any potential contamination from the pill. Carefully remove the pill from its packaging using clean fingers or tweezers. Avoid touching the pill as much as possible to prevent altering its taste or texture. 3. Place the pill inside the treat: Using care and precision, insert the pill into the center of the chosen treat. Ideally, the pill should be completely concealed within the treat, leaving no visible traces or residues. If necessary, moisten the treat slightly to help it adhere better around the pill. 4. Offer the disguised pill: Present the prepared treat to your cat as if it were a regular reward or snack. Encourage them to eat it by gently petting or speaking to them in a calm, soothing voice. Try not to appear anxious or tense, as this could alert your cat to something being amiss and make them more likely to reject the treat. 5. Monitor your cat's consumption: Keep a close eye on your cat while they consume the treat containing the hidden pill. It may take some time for them to finish it entirely, especially if they are suspicious of the new flavor or texture. Be patient and allow your cat to eat at their own pace. 6. Check for remnants: After your cat has finished eating, inspect their mouth and surrounding area for any signs of the pill remaining. You can do this by lightly holding their muzzle closed and gently rubbing their throat with your finger. If successful, your cat should swallow any residual pill particles along with their saliva. 7. Provide additional rewards: Once the pill has been safely administered, give your cat extra treats, praise, or affection as positive reinforcement. This can help create a positive association with taking medications and reduce future resistance or anxiety. 8. Clean up carefully: Dispose of any leftover treats or pill wrappers properly to prevent accidental ingestion by pets or children. Additionally, wash your hands again to eliminate any lingering residues or bacteria from handling the pill. 9. Maintain records: For ongoing medical treatments or multiple-day dosages, keep track of when you gave your cat their last dose. This information can help ensure consistent administration intervals and facilitate communication with veterinary professionals regarding treatment progress. Conclusion: While giving a cat a pill using Method II requires careful planning and execution, following these steps can significantly increase the likelihood of success. By utilizing tasty treats or foods as camouflage, you can minimize your cat's anxiety and discomfort associated with taking medications. With persistence, patience, and consistency, even the most finicky felines can learn to accept and tolerate pill administrations as part of their routine healthcare regimen. Where Food Comes From Kindergarten Oral Language Resources Children will: Learn about the concept of how food gets to the market. Access prior knowledge and build background about farmers and markets. Explore and apply the concept of how food gets to the market. Children will: Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of how food gets to the market. Orally use words that describe food, farmers, and markets. Extend oral vocabulary by speaking about food, farmers, and markets. Use key concept words [farmer, market]. Explain Use the slideshow to review the key concept words. Explain that children are going to learn about how food gets to the market: How food gets from a farm to a market. How food is grown on a farm by farmers What food is grown by farmers. Model After the host introduces the slideshow, point to the photo on screen. Ask children: What do you see in this picture? (fruit, vegetables) Ask children: How did these fruits and vegetables grow? (answers may vary). Say: Today we are going to learn about food, farmers, and markets. Many foods that we eat are grown on a farm and then sold in markets. What do you buy when you go to the market? (answers will vary). Guided Practice Guide children through the next four slides, showing them that food is grown on farms by farmers. Always have the children describe what they would like to grow. Apply Play the games that follow. Have them discuss with their partner the different topics that appear during the Talk About It feature. After the first game, ask children to talk about other fruits and vegetables grown on a farm. After the second game, have them discuss the difference between fruit and vegetables. Then have them give examples of each. Close Ask children: What are your favorite fruits and vegetables? Explain. Say: We would not be able to eat fruit and vegetables without a farmer growing them. Here your foot's in the way. Title: Enhance the Enjoyment of Fireworks: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Fireworks have been a source of entertainment and celebration for centuries. To maximize your enjoyment of fireworks displays, you can engage in various activities before, during, and after the event. This guide will walk you through 13 steps that will help deepen your appreciation for fireworks and create memorable experiences. Step 1: Research Local Fireworks Displays Before attending any fireworks display, research different events in your area. Look into factors such as location, date, time, cost (if applicable), accessibility, and crowd size. Choose an event that suits your preferences and needs. By doing so, you'll ensure a smooth start to your firework-viewing experience. Key Tips: * Check local government or tourism websites for official information about public fireworks shows. * Consider traveling further distances for larger, more extravagant displays if accessible. Step 2: Prepare Necessary Equipment Pack essential items like blankets, chairs, snacks, drinks, bug spray, sunscreen, and warm clothing. These items will enhance comfort and safety throughout the evening. Additionally, bring along binoculars or a camera with a zoom lens to capture intricate details of the fireworks. Key Guidelines: * Keep personal belongings organized using bags, baskets, or coolers. * Make sure cameras are fully charged and equipped with extra memory cards. Step 3: Arrive Early Plan to arrive at least an hour prior to the scheduled start time of the fireworks show. An early arrival guarantees prime viewing spots and allows ample time to set up seating arrangements and equipment. Key Tips: * Utilize online maps provided by organizers to scout out optimal locations. * Coordinate meeting times with friends and family members ahead of time. Step 4: Familiarize Yourself With Safety Measures Understand and adhere to all rules and regulations regarding fireworks safety. Obey designated smoking areas and refrain from bringing personal fireworks to professional displays. Furthermore, maintain a safe distance from launch sites and remain vigilant of potential hazards. Key Guidelines: * Follow instructions given by event staff and security personnel. * Teach children about proper fireworks etiquette and supervise them closely. Step 5: Engage in Social Activities Connect with fellow attendees by sharing stories, playing games, or participating in group activities. Building camaraderie strengthens the collective excitement surrounding the fireworks display. Key Tips: * Bring portable board games, frisbees, or other recreational items to pass the time. * Share food and beverages with those around you, fostering a sense of community. Step 6: Educate Yourself About Pyrotechnics Learn about the science behind fireworks and their historical significance. Understanding the artistry and skill involved in creating pyrotechnic effects enhances one's appreciation for the spectacle. Today, let's learn about a fun concept called "optimization." Imagine you have a big box of toys, but your mom tells you that you can only choose a certain number of toys to play with each day. You want to pick the toys that will give you the most amount of fun possible! But how do you decide which toys to choose? That's where optimization comes in handy. Optimization is like finding the best solution out of all the possible solutions. In math, we often talk about optimizing things like numbers or functions. Let's say you had a bunch of different numbers and you wanted to find the biggest one - that would be a simple kind of optimization problem. Or maybe you have a favorite ice cream shop that has lots of flavors, but you always spend too long deciding which one to get. You could make a rule to help you decide faster, like always choosing the flavor that starts with the same letter as your name. That way, you're optimizing your decision based on speed and personal preference! In the world of computer programming, people sometimes use special tools to help them solve optimization problems. One of those tools is called "MATLAB," which is a language that helps people write programs more easily. Sometimes, when people use MATLAB, they need to solve really complicated optimization problems, so they use something called "Yalmip," which is another tool that makes it easier to solve these complex problems. But even if you don't know how to use fancy tools like Yalmip yet, you can still practice solving optimization problems! Here are some ideas to try at home: * Pretend you have $5 to buy candy at the store. There are many types of candies to choose from, but you can only buy ones that cost less than $1 each. Which candies should you buy to get the most deliciousness per dollar spent? * Imagine you have a set of building blocks, and you want to build the tallest tower possible. How would you arrange the blocks to make sure your tower doesn't fall over? What shape would make the strongest base? * Suppose you love playing board games, but you never seem to win against your friends. Can you come up with a strategy that gives you the highest chance of winning, regardless of what moves your opponents make? Remember, optimization is all about making choices that lead to the best outcome. Keep practicing and soon you'll become an expert at finding the perfect solution every time! Title: How to Perform Boneless on a Tech Deck Introduction: Boneless is one of the most iconic skateboarding tricks that can also be performed using fingerboards like Tech Decks. It involves grabbing the board with your back hand while you jump over it, then returning your feet to the deck in mid-air before landing. This article will provide an easy-to-follow, illustrated guide on performing boneless on a Tech Deck. Materials Needed: * A Tech Deck or any other brand of fingerboard * Smooth surface (table, desk, etc.) Step 1: Familiarize Yourself with the Board Before attempting any trick, get comfortable holding and maneuvering the Tech Deck. Place both index fingers on top of the deck - one at the nose and another at the tail - so they rest parallel to the ground. Apply slight pressure downwards until you feel the wheels compress slightly; this gives more control during flip tricks. Make sure you're gripping firmly but not too tightly as it may hinder movement. Step 2: Positioning for Takeoff Place the Tech Deck on a flat surface and position yourself accordingly. Stand directly behind the board, bend your knees slightly, lean forward, and keep your balance centered above the deck. The goal here is to replicate the starting stance used when doing boneless on a full-size skateboard. Key Tip: Maintain good posture throughout all steps. Keeping your body aligned correctly makes executing moves easier and prevents potential injuries from sudden movements. Step 3: Grabbing the Trick Rail Now we move onto actually initiating the boneless motion. Lift your front foot off the board and slide it up towards the middle. As soon as your foot leaves the deck, swiftly bring your backhand under the board and grip its underside between your thumb and fingertips where the truck meets the deck. You have now successfully grabbed the 'trick rail'. Guideline: Practice sliding your foot along the deck without lifting it completely off yet. Once mastered, try lifting your foot higher each time until you reach the point described in Step 3. Remember to maintain balance and control over the board throughout these exercises. Step 4: Jumping Off With the board securely held by your backhand via the trick rail, push off gently with your standing leg to propel yourself into the air. Simultaneously release your grip on the board causing it to pop upward. Timing is crucial here; if done right, the board should rise just enough for you to perform the next action comfortably. Step 5: Landing Back On This part requires coordination and precision timing. While still airborne, quickly place your rear foot back onto the tail end of the deck followed closely by your leading foot which lands nearer the nose. Both feet need to make contact simultaneously for stability upon impact. Key Tips: Focus on controlling the descent rather than simply stomping downward. Also, ensure equal weight distribution across both trucks once landed. Conclusion: Performing boneless on a Tech Deck might seem challenging initially, especially for beginners. However, consistent practice combined with patience significantly improves skill level. Always remember safety first, even though fingerboarding poses minimal physical risk compared to traditional skateboarding. Enjoy experimenting with different styles, speeds, and heights! Chapter XI IPHIGENIA When Eleanor laid her head on her pillow that night, her mind was anxiously intent on some plan by which she might extricate her father from his misery; and, in her warm-hearted enthusiasm, self-sacrifice was decided on as the means to be adopted. Was not so good an Agamemnon worthy of an Iphigenia? She would herself personally implore John Bold to desist from his undertaking; she would explain to him her father's sorrows, the cruel misery of his position; she would tell him how her father would die if he were thus dragged before the public and exposed to such unmerited ignominy; she would appeal to his old friendship, to his generosity, to his manliness, to his mercy; if need were, she would kneel to him for the favour she would ask; but before she did this the idea of love must be banished. There must be no bargain in the matter. To his mercy, to his generosity, she could appeal; but as a pure maiden, hitherto even unsolicited, she could not appeal to his love, nor under such circumstances could she allow him to do so.
